label_comments.101B: MS BASIC-80 CRUNCH: tokenizes an input line. Scans the source text, folds reserved words to single-byte tokens via the reserved-word name table (DE=$08CF/$04D8 reserved-word pointers), passes through string literals ($22) and REM/DATA text verbatim, and emits the crunched line. Handles the GOTO/GOSUB ('GO TO'/'GO SUB') two-word forms. $0B15/$0B16 are CRUNCH mode flags.
label_comments.124F: [RE] CRUNCH output-byte helper: store A to the crunch buffer at (DE), advance DE, decrement remaining count BC; on buffer exhaustion raise error E=$17 (line/buffer overflow) via the $0D89 error dispatcher.
label_comments.13E4: MS BASIC CHRGET: INC HL then fetch the next program/text char at (HL) into A, skipping spaces, returning C set if it is a digit (0-9) and Z set at end-of-line ($00)/end-of-statement. Expands the embedded constant-token forms ($0B-$1E line-number/constant tokens, $1C/$1E etc.) into their literal values. Entry SUB_33C9_1 ($33CA) = CHRGOT (re-fetch current char without advancing).
label_comments.1CE7: [RE] Read char at (HL) and fold ASCII lowercase a-z ($61-$7A) to uppercase (AND $5F); leaves other chars unchanged. CRUNCH's case-insensitive keyword matcher uses this. SUB_3CCD ($3CCD) is the same fold applied to the char already in A.
label_comments.1A8C: MS BASIC-80 FRMEVL: evaluate a complete expression (numeric or string) at (HL) into the FAC ($0CB1). $3A71 calls SYNCHR-context entry; $3A76/$3A78 is the precedence-driven operator loop: fetch an operand (EVAL, SUB_3BF6), then while the next token is a binary operator of high enough precedence, recurse and apply. Relational/arithmetic operator tokens >= $EF are handled here; precedence table at $04ED, operator-function dispatch table at $0517/$0503.
label_comments.1C11: [RE] EVAL: fetch one operand/factor for FRMEVL. Parses a numeric constant (-> SUB_5F35 number scan), a parenthesized sub-expression, a string literal ($22), a variable reference, unary NOT ($E2)/minus, the FN call token ($E1), and the built-in function tokens (SCRN $CD/$EC/$ED, COLOR $D3, USR $E9, etc.) by dispatching to their FN_ handlers.
label_comments.1CC1: [RE] Evaluate a parenthesised expression / get a 16-bit integer argument: calls FRMEVL then converts the FAC to an integer in DE (ADD HL,HL). Used by functions and subscript evaluation.
label_comments.3BB3: [RE] Number/identifier scanner used by EVAL: reads a numeric literal (mantissa digits, decimal point, type suffixes % $ ! #) and records the value type in $0B14 ($02=single,$03=double,$04=string per MS convention); for an identifier it computes the type from the trailing %/$/!/# suffix. Feeds the constant into the FAC via the FP loader.
label_comments.2124: [RE] LIST de-tokenizer: expand a crunched line back to ASCII into a buffer (BC=dest, D=remaining length). Copies literal bytes, and for reserved-word tokens (>= $0B) looks the keyword name up (SUB_41E1) and copies its spelling. Used by LIST and by the sign-on/error formatting. $0C93 tracks inter-token spacing.
label_comments.2990: [RE] MS BASIC-80 floating-point multiply (FMULT): multiply the argument FP value by the FAC (mantissa at $0CB1, exponent $0CB4) using the shift-and-add mantissa loop, producing the product in the FAC.
label_comments.2874: [RE] FP add/normalize core (FADDT path): align exponents, add/subtract mantissas, and renormalize the result in the FAC ($0CB1/$0CB4). SUB_4C09 ($4C09) is the normalize/round tail that writes the final exponent to $0CB4.
label_comments.2BF4: [RE] LPOS() handler (function token $1A): current line-printer column (CALL $3DC8 = type-check helper). Also used as the integer-coerce entry.
label_comments.2C55: [RE] Store HL into the FAC low cells ($0CB1) and set the value-type to $02 (single) in $0B14 (SUB_4FDA). The MOVFR/integer-into-FAC primitive used throughout the evaluator.
label_comments.2CBA: [RE] FP mantissa right-shift / denormalize-align helper: shifts the FAC mantissa (B,C,D,E build the 4-byte mantissa) right by the exponent difference so two FP values can be added; common to FADD/FP_INT.
label_comments.3391: MS BASIC-80 FOUT: convert the FP value in the FAC ($0CB1) to a printable decimal ASCII string (sign $2B/$2D, digits, decimal point, E-exponent). Used by PRINT, STR$, and the sign-on free-bytes report. Value type read from $0B14.
label_comments.45A3: MS BASIC SYNCHR: verify the current char at (HL) equals the literal byte placed inline immediately after the CALL; if it matches, advance past it and CHRGET the next char; on mismatch JP to Syntax Error ($0D6F). The pervasive 'expect this token' primitive.
label_comments.45B5: [RE] RESTORE statement handler (token $8C): resets the DATA read pointer (optionally to a line number).
label_comments.449F: [RE] GETSTK/stack-room check: verify BC*2 bytes are available between SP and the top-of-storage pointer ($0B23); on failure fall through to SUB_6821_1 which raises 'Out of memory' (error E=$07) via the $0D89 dispatcher.
label_comments.2033: [RE] Variable-space overflow guard: if the free-space-remaining counter ($0844) has wrapped to 0, raise 'Out of memory' (E=$0C) via $0D89. Called before allocating variable/array storage.
label_comments.44E0: [RE] RUN/CLEAR setup: zero the array of work-pointers indexed by $0870 (file/FOR slots) starting at $0850, then fall through to clear variables. Entry from the warm-start path ($81BD).
label_comments.44F5: [RE] CLEARC: reset the variable, array and string-heap pointers ($0B57/$0B56/$0B6F, top-of-string), clearing all variables. The NEW/CLEAR/RUN re-initialization of the dynamic storage map.
label_comments.44C2: [RE] String free/space guard: if the requested string allocation would collide with the variable space, trigger garbage collection (SUB_6C82) and retry; if still no room raise 'Out of string space' (E=$07/$0E) via $0D89.
label_comments.48D6: [RE] String-space allocator (GETSPA): reserve A bytes at the top of the string heap (top-of-string pointer $0B48, string area base $0B73), invoking garbage collection on exhaustion; raises 'Out of string space' (E=$0E).
label_comments.4900: MS BASIC-80 GARBAG: garbage-collect / compact the string heap. Scans simple string variables, string arrays and string temporaries (pointers $0B23/$0B73/$0B27/$0B25) to find the highest still-referenced string and slide live strings up, reclaiming free space. Called by GETSPA when the heap is full.
label_comments.48BE: [RE] STROUT/print-message: print the NUL-terminated string at (HL) (or counted string) to the console one char at a time via OUTCHR ($6613), translating CR. Used for the sign-on banner and error messages.
label_comments.4291: [RE] OUTCHR: console character output with column tracking ($0837 cursor column), TAB ($09) expansion to 8-col stops, backspace ($08) and CR handling, then emits the byte through the BIOS console-out vector (SUB_666C, CALL into the runtime-patched $0000 cell).
label_comments.4300: [RE] High-level char-out with line-width/auto-CR logic: enforces the terminal width, expands TAB, handles backspace against the column counter ($0B11), and issues CRLF when the print column reaches the width. Wraps the OUTCHR primitive (SUB_6704).
label_comments.43DA: [RE] CONIN: read one console character via the BIOS console-in vector (CALL into the runtime-patched $0000 cell), mask to 7 bits, and service the Ctrl-O ($0F) output-suppress toggle ($083F). The keyboard input primitive.
label_comments.4437: [RE] Keyboard scan / pending-char handler (INKEY$ / Ctrl-C-Ctrl-S poll): reads a console char (SUB_675C), processes the Ctrl-S ($13) pause and Ctrl-C ($03) break ($0834 pending-key cell), and returns it. SUB_67B9_1 ($67CC) is the INKEY$ function evaluator.
label_comments.442C: [RE] RPC stub: CALL the runtime-patched 6502-bridge vector at $0000 (cell filled by cold-start from the CP/M BIOS jump table) to poll console status; returns Z per result. One of several $0000 RPC call sites the cold-start patcher fixes up.
label_comments.4406: [RE] Output CR ($0D) + LF ($0A) to the console (via OUTCHR), then clear pending auto-line state. The print-newline routine; used by the sign-on and after each Ok prompt.
label_comments.25C3: [RE] HOME statement handler (token $C7): Apple graphics superset -- clear the text screen / home cursor via the 6502 RPC.
label_comments.2602: [RE] 6502 remote-procedure-call dispatcher. HL = Apple monitor-ROM target address; stores it at A$VEC ($F3D0) then writes the trigger cell whose operand was self-modified at cold start to Z$CPU ($F3DE) (the 'LD ($0000),A' at $45EA is patched to 'LD ($F3DE),A' by the init at $8243). Storing to Z$CPU hands control to the 6502, which runs the Apple monitor routine and returns. This is the bridge graphics uses to reach the Apple ROM (TEXT/HOME/PREAD/etc.).
label_comments.25D9: [RE] GFX_ TEXT statement handler (token $C6): Apple graphics superset -- return the screen to text mode (RPC to the 6502 side).
label_comments.5E3B: [RE] Warm-start (READY/Ok) re-entry: re-init the program-end sentinel ($0846), then enter the direct-mode main loop. SUB_81AC_6 ($81C6) falls through to the immediate-statement executor at $0E23 if the start-up command pointer ($0850/$8350) is non-empty, else jumps to NEWSTT.
label_comments.5E44: [RE] Main-loop / Ok-prompt entry: load the deferred start-up command pointer ($0850 set by the cold-start /F /S /M command-tail parser); if it points at a non-empty command run it (SUB_7779, the direct-statement interpreter), otherwise JP $0E23 = NEWSTT main interpreter loop in low memory.
label_comments.5E51: [RE] Interpreter cold-start entry (the $1000 relocator JPs here after copying the body up to $3000). Initializes the runtime: BDOS handshake, RAM-top, the BASIC work cells, the RPC trigger patch (see $8240), and the console width from the SoftCard card config (see $827A).
label_comments.5F04: [RE] Select terminal line width during cold start: reads the configured console type ($F3BB) and sets the line-width work cell (SUB_4B20_12, $4B97) to 40 ($28) or the wide default, then initializes the file-control / disk-parameter pointers (SUB_4063).
label_comments.6050: [RE] Sign-on emit + free-bytes report: compute the free-byte count (top-of-mem minus program/var area), convert it to ASCII (FOUT, SUB_5713), and print the 'BASIC-80 Rev 5.2 ... Copyright ... Free' banner (STROUT, SUB_6C40) whose text is the data at $842F-$8482. Then sets the Ctrl-C handler vector ($0E34) and JP $81BD (warm start).
label_comments.60AD: [RE] Sign-on banner text + free-memory message strings (' . 5.2', CRLF, 'Copyright', 'BASIC-80 ... Version]', 'Created-', 'Bytes free' fragments). Consumed by COLD_SIGNON via STROUT; emitted as the LIST/print byte stream.
label_comments.13CC: [RE] Statement executor / dispatch. Token in A; SUB $81; if <0 not a statement; CP $5B reject tokens above the table; RLCA (index*2); index the statement-handler DEFW table at $0108 (addr = $0108 + (token-$81)*2), load handler into BC, PUSH BC, fall into CHRGET ($33C9) and RET to the handler. The graphics statement handlers (HOME..PLOT, tokens $C7-$D5) live in this table at $0194-$01B0.
label_comments.13DB: [RE] LD HL,$0108 / ADD HL,BC / LD C,(HL)/INC HL/LD B,(HL): fetches the statement handler address from the DEFW dispatch table (base $0108, index = (token-$81)*2).
label_comments.2554: [RE] Clears the screen reverse/INVERSE flag cell ($0CB6=0) used by the console attribute path. Entry just below the VTAB/HTAB cursor helpers.
label_comments.255B: [RE] VTAB statement handler (token $C8): Apple graphics superset -- set the text cursor row (CALL $458E reads the operand).
label_comments.256F: [RE] Cursor/position helper used by HTAB/VTAB/HOME. Reads the per-screen cursor-config cells SLTTYP table at $F396/$F397 (40 vs 80-column geometry; bit7 selects swap of H/L) and folds the BASIC position ($0B11) into a console call via $6704. $F396/$F397 are SoftCard I/O-config screen cells in the $F3xx block.
label_comments.2590: [RE] Emits one cursor-position component. Indexes the $F397 screen-config cell by E, applies the bit7 'present' test (AND $7F), and routes the value through the console output routine $6704. Part of the HTAB/VTAB cursor positioning path.
label_comments.25A9: [RE] Evaluate one expression and return it as an 8-bit value (CALL FRMEVL-byte $4097); A=0 -> error ($34D0), else returns A-1. Argument fetch shared by the cursor/graphics statements.
label_comments.25C1: [RE] HOME statement (token $C7, dispatch $0194 -> $45A8). $45A6 JRs into $4539_3; $45A8 zeroes the BASIC cursor-position cell $0B11, selects the screen attribute table entry (E=1, BC=$051E/$041E mode index) and calls SCREEN_POS_EMIT to clear/home the text cursor.
label_comments.25EA: Soft-switch reads: LD A,($E051) = Apple $C051 TXTSET (text mode ON); LD A,($E054) = Apple $C054 page-1 select (LOWSCR). Part of STMT_TEXT, returns the display to the text screen.
label_comments.2605: [RE] Self-modified store. Assembled as LD ($0000),A; cold-start init ($8240-$8245) reads Z$CPU ($F3DE) and patches this operand so it becomes LD (Z$CPU),A -- the write that actually triggers the 6502 to service the call queued at A$VEC.
label_comments.265D: [RE] COLOR statement handler (token $CD): Apple graphics superset -- set the low-res plotting color.
label_comments.275E: [RE] VPOS() handler (function token $34): Apple graphics superset -- current text cursor row (CALL $409A).
label_comments.27E0: [RE] Graphics display-mode soft-switch helper. LD ($E050),A = $C050 TXTCLR (graphics on); LD HL,$E053; RRA selects mixed vs full screen: NC -> use $E053 ($C053 MIXSET, mixed text+graphics, D=$28 rows); C -> DEC L to $E052 ($C052 MIXCLR, full-screen graphics, D=$30 rows); the LD (HL),L touches the chosen switch. Sets the Apple display into the requested graphics mode.
label_comments.27E1: Soft-switch write: LD ($E050),A = Apple $C050 TXTCLR (turn the text overlay off / graphics on). First switch touched by GFX_SET_DISPLAY_MODE.
label_comments.5EBE: [RE] Cold-start install of the 6502 RPC path. LD HL,($F3DE) reads Z$CPU (the SoftCard CPU-switch / call-trigger cell) and writes it into the self-modified operand of RPC_TRIGGER_STORE ($45EB), so every later RPC_CALL writes to Z$CPU to hand off to the 6502. Also seeds the $0001 vector with the BASIC warm restart ($4B7A).
label_comments.5EF8: [RE] Cold-start console-width select from the SoftCard card type. LD A,($F3BB) reads SLTTYP+2 (the screen/card descriptor in the $F3xx I/O-config block): value 3 or 2 picks the 80-column geometry, else default $28 (40 columns). Result stored in the width cell $4B97 used by the cursor/screen code. [DOC: SoftCard I/O config / Card Type Table -- 40 vs 80 column console.]
label_comments.45D4: [RE] END statement handler (token $81). Reached via the GONE/NEWSTT statement dispatcher at $33B1 (SUB $81; RLCA; LD HL,$0108; ADD HL,BC; load handler; JP).
label_comments.12AA: [RE] FOR statement handler (token $82): sets up the FOR/NEXT loop frame on the runtime stack.
label_comments.4750: [RE] NEXT statement handler (token $83): advances/closes the current FOR loop frame.
label_comments.15CF: [RE] DATA statement handler (token $84): no-op at run time (scanned/skipped). COMMON (token $B3) also dispatches to this same entry.
label_comments.1913: [RE] INPUT statement handler (token $85): prompt + read console line, parse values into the variable list.
label_comments.19D2: [RE] READ statement handler (token $87): reads the next DATA item into a variable.
label_comments.15F6: [RE] LET / implicit-assignment handler (token $88): evaluates RHS (CALL $5F35) and stores into the target variable.
label_comments.1560: [RE] GOTO statement handler (token $89): parses a line number, FNDLIN search, sets the text pointer.
label_comments.1534: [RE] RUN statement handler (token $8A): clears variables and begins execution at the start (or a given line).
label_comments.1548: [RE] GOSUB statement handler (token $8D): pushes a return frame then transfers like GOTO.
label_comments.15B4: [RE] RETURN statement handler (token $8E): pops the GOSUB return frame and resumes.
label_comments.45CF: [RE] STOP statement handler (token $90): break to direct mode with a Break message (shares logic with END at $6956).
label_comments.46C6: [RE] CLEAR statement handler (token $92): clears variables/strings and optionally sets memory/stack limits.
label_comments.20C6: [RE] LIST statement handler (token $93): detokenizes program lines to the console (uses the reserved-word table walk at $4178).
label_comments.44F4: [RE] NEW statement handler (token $94): erases the current program and variables.
label_comments.165D: [RE] ON statement handler (token $95): ON..GOTO/GOSUB/ERROR computed branch (CP $A4 tests for ERROR).
label_comments.1EA1: [RE] DEF statement handler (token $96): DEF FN user-function definition / DEF USR (CP $E1 = USR token).
label_comments.22CC: [RE] POKE statement handler (token $97): evaluate address,value then store to memory.
label_comments.4629: [RE] CONT statement handler (token $98): resume a stopped program from the saved text pointer ($0B6D).
label_comments.175D: [RE] LPRINT statement handler (token $9B): PRINT directed to the line printer; falls into the shared PRINT engine.
label_comments.20C1: [RE] LLIST statement handler (token $9C): LIST directed to the line printer (sets printer flag then joins LIST).
label_comments.2062: [RE] WIDTH statement handler (token $9D): set console/printer output line width (CP $9B tests for LPRINT form).
label_comments.463D: [RE] TRACE statement handler (token $9F): enable execution trace (TRON-equivalent); sets the trace flag.
label_comments.463E: [RE] NOTRACE statement handler (token $A0): disable execution trace (XOR A then store the trace flag).
label_comments.4643: [RE] SWAP statement handler (token $A1): exchange the values of two variables.
label_comments.4681: [RE] ERASE statement handler (token $A2): delete a dimensioned array, freeing its storage.
label_comments.3EE8: [RE] EDIT statement handler (token $A3): enter the line editor for a program line.
label_comments.16E1: [RE] ERROR statement handler (token $A4): force the given error code through the ERROR handler.
label_comments.16A7: [RE] RESUME statement handler (token $A5): return from an ON ERROR handler (RESUME/RESUME NEXT/RESUME line).
label_comments.228A: [RE] DELETE statement handler (token $A6): delete a range of program lines (CALL $0F61 parses the range).
label_comments.22FD: [RE] RENUM statement handler (token $A8): renumber program lines (defaults start/step in the LD BC,$000A).
label_comments.149F: [RE] DEFSTR statement handler (token $A9): declare a default-string letter range. DEFINT/DEFSNG/DEFDBL ($AA-$AC) enter a few bytes later with a different type code.
label_comments.18A6: [RE] LINE statement handler (token $AD): LINE INPUT (read a whole console line into a string).
label_comments.1596: [RE] POP statement handler (token $AE): discard the top GOSUB return frame.
label_comments.4E1C: [RE] WHILE statement handler (token $AF): begin a WHILE/WEND loop; records the loop text pointer ($0B4E).
label_comments.4E3D: [RE] WEND statement handler (token $B0): test the WHILE condition and loop or fall through.
label_comments.4EAE: [RE] CALL statement handler (token $B1): call an external machine-code routine.
label_comments.5206: [RE] WRITE statement handler (token $B2): PRINT a comma-separated, quoted list to console/file.
label_comments.4F37: [RE] CHAIN statement handler (token $B4): load and run another program, optionally preserving variables.
label_comments.242E: [RE] OPTION statement handler (token $B5): OPTION BASE 0/1 array lower-bound selector.
label_comments.2478: [RE] RANDOMIZE statement handler (token $B6): reseed the RND generator (prompts for a seed if none given).
label_comments.5A64: [RE] SYSTEM statement handler (token $B7): exit GBASIC back to CP/M (shares the RET NZ pattern of the simple stubs).
label_comments.5971: [RE] OPEN statement handler (token $B8): open a disk file on a channel.
label_comments.5559: [RE] FIELD statement handler (token $B9): define random-file buffer field variables.
label_comments.5BDF: [RE] PUT statement handler (token $BB): write a random-file record. GET (token $BA) enters one byte later at $7F62.
label_comments.551C: [RE] CLOSE statement handler (token $BC): close one or all open file channels.
label_comments.54A7: [RE] MERGE statement handler (token $BE): merge an ASCII program file into the current program.
label_comments.591E: [RE] NAME statement handler (token $C0): rename a disk file (NAME old AS new).
label_comments.55BB: [RE] RSET statement handler (token $C3): right-justify a string into a FIELD buffer variable. LSET (token $C2) enters at $793E with the justify flag cleared.
label_comments.54E0: [RE] SAVE statement handler (token $C4): save the program to disk (tokenized or ASCII).
label_comments.5A6E: [RE] RESET statement handler (token $C5): close all files / reset the disk system.
label_comments.25B2: [RE] HTAB statement handler (token $C9): Apple graphics superset -- set the text cursor column.
label_comments.2609: [RE] GR statement handler (token $CC): Apple graphics superset -- enter low-res graphics mode (loads mode byte then 6502 RPC).
label_comments.2693: [RE] HLIN statement handler (token $CE): Apple graphics superset -- draw a horizontal low-res line.
label_comments.2711: [RE] BEEP statement handler (token $D4): Apple graphics superset -- sound the console bell via the 6502 RPC.
label_comments.273D: [RE] WAIT statement handler (token $D5): poll an I/O port until (in AND mask) XOR xor is non-zero.
label_comments.4B03: [RE] LEFT$() handler (function token $01): leftmost n chars of a string.
label_comments.4B0C: [RE] RIGHT$() handler (function token $02): rightmost n chars of a string.
label_comments.2CF8: [RE] SGN() handler (function token $04): sign of a number (-1/0/+1).
label_comments.2AE7: [RE] INT() handler (function token $05): floor to integer.
label_comments.3A18: [RE] SQR() handler (function token $07): square root (MBF math package).
label_comments.3AB8: [RE] RND() handler (function token $08): pseudo-random number (reads the seed at $0CB4).
label_comments.294A: [RE] SIN() handler (function token $09): sine (MBF; shares the poly evaluator $47C5).
label_comments.3B5A: [RE] TAN() handler (function token $0D): tangent (MBF; shares poly evaluator $47C5).
label_comments.4A67: [RE] POS() handler (function token $10): current console column (LD BC,$3E32 = return-integer helper).
label_comments.4A73: [RE] VAL() handler (function token $13): numeric value of a string (FIN).
label_comments.4A83: [RE] ASC() handler (function token $14): character code of a string's first byte.
label_comments.22C2: [RE] CHR$() handler (function token $15): one-character string from a code.
label_comments.4ABA: [RE] PEEK() handler (function token $16): read a memory byte (CALL $409A evaluates the address).
label_comments.482D: [RE] SPACE$() handler (function token $17): string of n spaces.
label_comments.2C6C: [RE] CINT() handler (function token $1B): coerce to integer.
label_comments.2C98: [RE] CSNG() handler (function token $1C): coerce to single precision.
label_comments.2CE5: [RE] CDBL() handler (function token $1D): coerce to double precision.
label_comments.52DF: [RE] LOF() handler (function token $30): length-of-file in records (LD A,$02 selects the file-info op).
label_comments.2707: [RE] MKD$() handler (function token $33): pack a double into an 8-byte string for random files (note: this entry sits in the Apple graphics block region).
label_comments.26ED: [RE] PDL() handler (function token $35): Apple graphics superset -- read a game paddle/analog value via the 6502 RPC.
label_comments.0C98: [RE] In-RAM call trampoline: POP return addr into HL, restore AF/DE off the stack, JP (HL). Re-enters caller with saved A/flags and DE.
label_comments.0D15: Data: 'Break' message + CRLF used by the STOP/Ctrl-C path ($0E0B). $0CED ' in ' suffix, $0CF7 'Break'.
label_comments.0D20: [RE] Entry to FOR/GOSUB stack-frame fixup: HL = SP+4, fall into STKFRAME_SCAN to walk the runtime stack.
label_comments.0D24: [RE] Walk runtime stack frames: skip FOR frames (token $AF, +16/+6 bytes), find GOSUB markers ($82), compare each frame's text pointer vs HL via FNDLIN-cmp ($691F) to fix up frames after a program edit/delete.
label_comments.0D51: [RE] CONT command: if saved resume ptr $0844 is -1 (no CONTinue context) or trace flag $0B68 set, raise error E=$13 ('Can't continue'); else JP $6969 to resume.
label_comments.0D64: Named-error entry stubs: each loads an error code into E (LD E,n via the $1E opcode of the next LD BC) then falls through to ERROR_DISPATCH at $0D89. Overlapping table of error vectors.
label_comments.0D8C: [RE] Restore saved program pointer ($0B50 -> $0844) on the no-continue path before re-entering the error/ready flow.
label_comments.0D92: Syntax-error entry: LD E,$02 then fall through the coded-error table into ERROR_DISPATCH ($0D89). Common target of statement parsers (JP $0D6F).
label_comments.0DAC: ERROR handler: saves current text ptr to $0B60, clears trap state ($0C9A/$0CA0), records resume line $0B62, then prints '?<msg> Error[ in line]' and returns to direct mode via $68F4.
label_comments.0DC1: [RE] Build/print the error text: emit '?', look up the 2-char error mnemonic, append ' Error', and optionally ' in <line>'; falls into the direct-mode READY path.
label_comments.0DCA: Packed table of 2-letter error mnemonics (NF/SN/RG/OD/FC/OV/OM/UL/BS/DD/...) indexed by error code, printed by ERROR_PRINT_MSG.
label_comments.0E23: [RE] After error message: if current line is direct (text begins '?'), point at the canned direct-mode message ($0521); else print ' in <line>' and drop to READY.
label_comments.0E2E: [RE] STOP/Ctrl-C break: print 'Break' message ($6C40 STROUT), compute/print the current line number, then fall into the READY prompt and NEWSTT main loop.
label_comments.0E46: READY/main interpreter loop: print prompt char, clear output column $083F, read a console line ($781A), then process it (tokenize or execute).
label_comments.0E61: [RE] Process the input line: FOUT the leading line number, FNDLIN to locate it, CRUNCH-tokenize ($3000), then insert/replace/delete in the program or execute as a direct statement.
label_comments.0E9E: [RE] Verify the new/edited program line fits below string space: add length to text end, compare vs available memory ($691F), error 'Out of memory' ($6443) if it overflows.
label_comments.0EC9: [RE] Execute a tokenized direct-mode statement: CHRGET-prime and call the statement dispatcher; on a bare line number fall to edit/insert.
label_comments.0EDA: [RE] Insert/replace a program line: delete any existing same-numbered line, make room by moving text up ($6811 GETSTK), splice the crunched line in, then relink.
label_comments.0F3C: [RE] After an edit: reset DATA ptr, rechain program-line link pointers (CHEAD path), run the variable/CLEAR reset ($688D), then re-enter NEWSTT.
label_comments.0F5C: [RE] Relink program lines from program start ($0846): rebuild each line's forward-link word so the chain is contiguous after an insert/delete.
label_comments.0F60: [RE] CHEAD scan loop: for each line walk to its terminating $00, fold embedded tokens, and write the next-line link pointer; stop at the program's double-zero end marker.
label_comments.0F84: [RE] Parse an optional 'start[-end]' line-number range (LIST/DELETE): get first line# ($34D5), accept ',' / '-' ($F3) separator, get second; syntax-error to $0D6F on malformed input.
label_comments.0FA9: [RE] FNDLIN entry that takes the line number already in DE (via the stack) before searching the program.
label_comments.0FAB: Find a program line by number: scan the linked line list from program start ($0846), comparing each line number against the target; returns C set/clear and BC=prior link for the insert point.
label_comments.0FAE: FNDLIN inner loop: follow each line's forward link, load its line number, FNDLIN-compare vs target; return when found or when the number passes the target.
label_comments.0FCA: [RE] Evaluate a PRINT/INPUT-style item with optional '#'(file/channel $23) prefix: CHRGOT, FRMEVL the operand ($3A75), branch on numeric vs string ($3DC8), and stage it into the string/var area ($0B6F).
label_comments.1097: [RE] CRUNCH continuation reached via LD BC,L_307C/PUSH BC/RET (executable, rendered as DEFB): finishes tokenizing the reserved word that SUB_6A41 matched, emitting its token byte via CRUNCH_EMIT and looping back to CRUNCH_1.
label_comments.124D: [RE] Load A=':' ($3A) then fall into CRUNCH_EMIT: emit a statement-separator colon into the crunch buffer (used around tokens like ELSE/REM that imply a colon).
label_comments.129A: [RE] CRUNCH helper: walk HL backward over trailing whitespace (space/$09/$0A), then INC HL to leave HL just past the last non-blank; used to trim blanks before re-scanning a number/reserved word.
label_comments.13E5: CHRGOT: re-fetch the current text char at (HL) into A without advancing (CHRGET minus the leading INC HL); sets C if digit, Z at end-of-line/statement; expands embedded constant tokens $0B-$1E.
label_comments.1460: [RE] CHRGOT constant-token tail: for the embedded numeric-constant tokens ($0B-$1E) decoded by CHRGOT, materialise the literal value into FAC ($0CB1/$0CB3) and value-type ($0B14), then resume the char scan at CHRGOT_3 ($33E7).
label_comments.14F0: [RE] LINGET entry handling the '.' shortcut: if current char is '.' ($2E) substitute the current line number from $0B62 and CHRGET past it; otherwise fall into LINGET to parse an explicit decimal line number into DE.
label_comments.14FB: LINGET: DEC HL then parse a decimal line number from the text at (HL) into DE (range-checked); standard MS BASIC line-number reader used by GOTO/GOSUB/ON/RESUME/THEN.
label_comments.14FC: LINGET entry without the leading DEC HL: CHRGET the first char then parse the decimal line number into DE (LINGET re-entry used by ON-GOTO list scanning).
label_comments.1506: [RE] LINGET digit-accumulation loop: read ASCII digits, DE = DE*10 + digit (via the *10 sequence and $1998 overflow guard at SUB_691F) until a non-digit; returns the parsed line number in DE.
label_comments.16EC: [RE] Parse an optional line-number range (default span $000A) via LINGET_DOT/LINGET, store the start/end pointers into the continue/trace cells $0B5A/$0B57/$0B58, then JP $0E3E to re-enter the NEWSTT main loop at that line.
label_comments.171F: [RE] IF statement handler: evaluate the condition via FRMEVL, skip an optional ',' and the THEN/GOTO token; if true, a following line-number token ($0E) means GOTO (JP STMT_GOTO) else execute the THEN clause via NEWSTT_DISPATCH; if false, scan forward over the matching ELSE ($9E) depth (SUB_3704_4) to the alternate/next line.
label_comments.1765: PRINT statement engine (shared by PRINT/LPRINT/PRINT#): walk the print list emitting expressions, honour ',' tab zones and ';' (no-space), TAB($E8)/SPC($DF)/USING($E3) functions, and emit CRLF unless suppressed; column tracking via $0837/$0B11.
label_comments.189A: [RE] PRINT epilogue/reset: clear the LPRINT-direction flag $0838 and the file/device pointer $0840 back to console defaults at the end of a PRINT statement.
label_comments.18D2: INPUT error literal "?Redo from start" + CR/LF; STROUT'd by the INPUT re-prompt path (STMT_LINE_2) when the user's typed reply does not parse. Bytes after the terminator are INPUT-path code rendered as DEFB.
label_comments.191F: [RE] INPUT prompt parser: if the next token is '"' read the quoted prompt string and emit it; the following ';' vs ',' sets the suppress-'?'-mark flag ($0C94) and trailing-comma flag ($083F); shared prompt setup before reading the console line.
label_comments.1947: [RE] INPUT continuation (executable, rendered as DEFB): emits the '? ' prompt via OUTCHR, reads the console line (SUB_702B), and on empty/abort branches to STMT_END; falls through to the value-parse loop L_394D.
label_comments.1968: [RE] INPUT value-parse loop (executable, rendered as DEFB): for each variable, read a field from the typed line (honouring quotes and ',' separators), convert and assign via STMT_LET_2; on mismatch jumps to the '?Redo from start' re-prompt (STMT_LINE_1/2).
label_comments.1A85: [RE] Evaluate-expression wrapper: SYNCHR the current char (advance past a required token), RET if at end-of-statement (P flag), else JP into FRMEVL to evaluate the following expression into FAC.
label_comments.1A91: [RE] FRMEVL body entry with D=$00 (lowest operator precedence); falls into the precedence loop. Canonical MS BASIC-80 FRMEVL after the SYNCHR-context check.
label_comments.1A93: [RE] FRMEVL operator-precedence loop: save pending-operator precedence (D), check stack, fetch one operand (FRMEVL_EVAL_OPERAND); then while the next token at (HL) is a binary operator (>= $EF) of precedence > pending, recurse here and apply. Relational vs arithmetic vs string-concat dispatch follows.
label_comments.1B1C: [RE] Relational-operator collector: tokens $EF-$F1 (=,<,>) accumulate a 3-bit relation mask in D (RLA/XOR), advancing past consecutive relation tokens via CHRGET; falls to FRMEVL_ARITHOP when a non-relation operator is seen.
label_comments.1B51: [RE] Arithmetic/string binary-operator apply: if pending precedence (B) < new operator precedence ($64 guard) push the FAC operand and recurse; sets up the operator-result combine via FRMEVL_OPCOMBINE.
label_comments.1B6D: [RE] Operator-result combine: after the right operand is evaluated, recover operator code/type ($0B15/$0B14), coerce both operands to a common numeric type (CINT/CSNG paths) or take the string path ($08), then dispatch to the arithmetic operator routine via the $0517 (numeric) / $0503 (relational) vector tables.
label_comments.1DAC: [RE] Indexed vector dispatch: ADD HL,BC then load the 16-bit target from (HL) and JP (HL). Shared by the operator-function table ($04F9) and other token-dispatch sites.
label_comments.1DB2: [RE] Pre-scan a leading unary operator token: recognises NOT ($F3), unary minus ($2D)/$F2 and unary plus ($2B), toggling D; backs up (HL) one char when none match. Used when fetching a factor/operand.
label_comments.1DE3: [RE] Classify the FAC value type from $0B14 (2=sng,3=dbl,4=str,8=int): returns Z when string and sets carry/flags so callers can branch numeric-vs-string. Canonical MS BASIC type-test helper.
label_comments.1E3B: [RE] 16-bit integer subtract (HL := HL - DE) then store the integer result into the FAC via FP_STORE (SUB_51EE).
label_comments.1E49: [RE] Load print-column counter ($0B11)+1 as an integer into the FAC (entry for POS-style functions); shares the tail with FP_LOAD_INT_TO_FAC.
label_comments.1E4D: [RE] Store the 8-bit value in A (zero-extended to HL) as an integer into the FAC via FP_STORE_FAC_INT.
label_comments.1E72: [RE] Compute the address of a USR(n) dispatch vector: base $081F + (digit index*2 from $0B1B if a numeric suffix follows the USR token), returned in DE. Used by DEF USR / USR-call setup.
label_comments.201A: [RE] Apply a binary operator: mask the operator code (AND $07), index the operator-routine vector table at $04F9, and jump via DISPATCH_VECTOR_HLBC, preserving HL across the call.
label_comments.202E: [RE] Copy BC bytes from (DE) to (HL), ascending (LDI-style hand loop). General memory-move helper used by DEF FN parameter save/restore and variable-table shuffling.
label_comments.2041: [RE] Fetch and validate a variable-name token after SYNCHR: requires an alphabetic first char (JP PO on letter test), records the name in $0B52, then resolves it via PTRGET (SUB_5F30_2).
label_comments.207E: [RE] WIDTH continuation: parse 'WIDTH #file,width' branch - stores file-width to $083B/$083D, then handles optional ',pos' second field.
label_comments.2096: [RE] WIDTH helper: fold the requested column count into the valid 1..n range by repeated SUB $0E, then bias by E (original value); returns the clamped width byte.
label_comments.20A0: [RE] MS BASIC GETINT entry: advance the text pointer (CHRGET) then evaluate a numeric expression and return it as a 16-bit integer (falls into GETINT).
label_comments.20A3: [RE] MS BASIC GETINT: evaluate expression at the text pointer (FRMEVL), convert FAC to signed 16-bit (FN_LPOS) into DE; flags set from high byte.
label_comments.20A6: [RE] Convert current numeric value (FAC) to a 16-bit integer in DE via FN_LPOS; A=high byte, OR A sets Z if value fits in one byte (used by GETBYT/POKE/PEEK).
label_comments.20AF: [RE] MS BASIC GETBYT entry: advance the text pointer (CHRGET) then fall into GETBYT (0..255 byte evaluator).
label_comments.20B2: [RE] MS BASIC GETBYT: evaluate expression (FRMEVL) then fall into CONINT to range-check it as a 0..255 byte in A/E.
label_comments.20B5: [RE] MS BASIC CONINT: require the integer to fit in one byte - convert via FRC_INT_DE and 'Illegal function call' (SUB_34CC_1) if the high byte is non-zero; returns the byte in A and E.
label_comments.211B: [RE] Print a $00-terminated message byte-by-byte through SUB_6800 (console out); used by LIST to emit the post-line trailer text at $0A0E.
label_comments.21FC: [RE] LIST detokenizer helper: classify the next program byte - returns NC for an alpha/keyword char, C (with CCF) for ASCII digit 0-9, controlling whether DETOKENIZE_LINE treats it as a reserved-word token or literal.
label_comments.22AF: [RE] Copy a string from (DE) into the string pool growing at $0B6F, byte-by-byte via SUB_691F until done; updates the $0B6F string-area pointer. Used by DELETE/edit to relocate text.
label_comments.22E1: [RE] MS BASIC GETADR: evaluate FAC and convert to an unsigned 16-bit address in BC/$9180-bias form (for POKE/PEEK); rejects out-of-range via SUB_4BA6. Pushes FN_LPOS as the integer-fetch continuation.
label_comments.238D: [RE] RENUM pass 2: walk every program line, find line-number references after GOTO/GOSUB/THEN tokens ($A4/$0E markers), translate each old line number to its new value (SUB_34EB lookup) and rewrite the 3-byte encoded line-number token in place; reports 'Undefined line' for missing targets.
label_comments.241D: [RE] Write the 3-byte encoded line-number token (marker A, line# in BC) backward into the program text just before the pointer in $0B17.
label_comments.2426: [RE] If the renumber-pending flag $0B56 is set, run the line-reference fix-up pass (RENUM_PATCH_LINEREFS); otherwise return. Called after DELETE edits the program.
label_comments.246A: [RE] Print a $00-terminated string at (HL) through STROUT_PUTC_SAVE, preserving registers; loops to end of string.
label_comments.2474: [RE] Emit one character (A) to the console via OUTDO (OUTDO_WIDTH_1) while preserving AF across the call.
label_comments.24CD: [RE] Enter the structured-block program scanner with delimiter set for WHILE/WEND (C=$1D); used to balance nested block-statement keywords while searching forward through program text.
label_comments.24D1: [RE] Structured-block program scanner (FOR/NEXT default, C=$1A): walk crunched program text counting nesting of the matching open/close keyword tokens (e.g. FOR vs NEXT, $82/$83), tracking the current line pointer in $0C71; returns when the balancing close at depth 0 is found.
label_comments.2657: [RE] Low-res block-draw RPC tail: LD HL,$F819 (6502 HLIN handler entry) then JP RPC_CALL. Called by GR fill, HLIN setup to execute the horizontal-segment draw on the 6502 side.
label_comments.2664: [RE] COLOR statement body: validate color E<16, replicate it into both nibbles (4x ADD A,A; OR E) and store the packed low-res color byte to $F030.
label_comments.2672: [RE] GFX parse line coordinates: returns A = cross-axis coord, E = start, D = end.
label_comments.26AA: [RE] VLIN statement body (mirror of GFX_STMT_HLIN): parse coords, store Y0/Y1->$F045/$F047 and X->$F02D, then fall to the low-res draw RPC ($F828) via SUB_46C6_1.
label_comments.26C1: [RE] Parse two comma-separated byte expressions (X then Y): eval first into A, SYNCHR ',', eval second into A; preserves DE across. Used by PLOT/SCRN/BEEP coordinate reads.
label_comments.26CE: [RE] Parse+range-check a low-res PLOT coordinate pair: X<$30 and Y<$28 (else FC error), store X->$F045 and Y->$F047.
label_comments.26E1: [RE] PLOT statement body: parse+validate coords (GFX_PARSE_PLOT_COORD), set HL=$F800 (6502 PLOT handler) and fall into the RPC tail.
label_comments.2702: [RE] Store a sign-extended byte (A, sign already in carry from RLA/SBC A,A) as a 16-bit integer in HL and JP FP_STORE_FAC_INT. Result tail for PDL().
label_comments.2816: [RE] FADDT entry: load constant pointer (L_5BDC) then fall into FADD-with-operand; adds the FP value at (HL) to FAC.
label_comments.2819: [RE] FADD with operand at (HL): load the addend mantissa/exp into regs (SUB_4EB8) then align-and-add against FAC.
label_comments.2821: [RE] FADD entry when addend already supplied; loads FAC into HL-pair (SUB_4E76 negate path) then aligns exponents.
label_comments.2824: [RE] FADD core: compare exponents of addend (B) and FAC ($0CB4); if FAC=0 just store addend; shift the smaller mantissa right to align, then add.
label_comments.2871: [RE] FADD mantissa combine: CALL C,FCOMPL (sign disagreement) then drop into FADD proper to add/subtract the aligned mantissas.
label_comments.2887: [RE] MBF normalize/round tail: left-justify the summed mantissa (B=shift count), adjust the FAC exponent at $0CB4, round, store result back (JP FADD_STORE).
label_comments.28C8: [RE] Round/carry propagation: bump mantissa bytes E,D,C and exponent on a rounding carry; overflow to error if the exponent wraps.
label_comments.28D5: [RE] 3-byte mantissa add: (HL..)+E,D,C with carry into the FAC mantissa registers.
label_comments.28E1: [RE] FCOMPL: two's-complement negate the working mantissa (B,E,D,C) and flip the sign byte at $0CB5; used when adding operands of opposite sign.
label_comments.28F5: [RE] Byte-granular mantissa right-shift: shift the 4-byte mantissa right whole bytes by (exponent diff)/8, then fall into the bit-shift remainder.
label_comments.2917: [RE] Bit-granular mantissa right-shift: rotate C,D,E,B right one bit per step to finish exponent alignment.
label_comments.295D: [RE] SIN range reduction / polynomial preprocessor: folds the argument into the principal interval before the Chebyshev poly (CALL $5D65 poly eval); helper for FN_SIN.
label_comments.29E8: [RE] FDIV reciprocal-divide setup: pushes the divisor (SUB_4E9A) and constant L_5384, then falls into FDIV restoring-division.
label_comments.29F3: [RE] MBF floating-point divide: divisor self-modified into the subtract trio ($4D9D/$4DA1/$4DA5), restoring long division of the FAC mantissa, result normalized via FADD_NORMALIZE.
label_comments.2A77: [RE] MUL/DIV sign+exponent combine: XOR the two operand sign bytes ($0CC0/$0CC1) and add the biased exponents; produces the result sign/exponent for FMUL and FDIV.
label_comments.2A84: [RE] Add operand exponent (B) to FAC exponent at $0CB4 with overflow/underflow detection; jumps to over/underflow handlers; shared by FMUL/FDIV.
label_comments.2A9F: [RE] Exponent-result finalize: pop saved sign, branch to zero result (FADD clear) on underflow or overflow error on overflow.
label_comments.2AA1: [RE] Over/underflow decision: re-test FAC sign (SUB_4E47), complement, and route to zero-result vs overflow-error.
label_comments.2AAE: [RE] Scale FAC by a small power of two: load FAC, add 2 to the exponent (x4), renormalize via FADD_ALIGN, bump $0CB4; used by SIN/EXP poly scaling.
label_comments.2AC5: [RE] SIGN of FAC: returns A=0 if exponent($0CB4)=0, else A=$01 (positive) or $FF (negative) from the sign byte $0CB3.
label_comments.2AD4: [RE] Float the signed byte in A into the FAC: set exponent $88, clear low mantissa, set sign, normalize via FADD_COMBINE.
label_comments.2AEB: [RE] Negate-FAC-and-continue: toggle the FAC sign (SUB_4E76) then re-dispatch through the integer/type check ($3DC8) path.
label_comments.2AF4: [RE] Negate FAC: flip the high (sign) bit of the FAC sign byte at $0CB3.
label_comments.2AFF: [RE] Store signed 16-bit value in A (sign-extended into HL) into the FAC as an integer (JP FP_STORE_FAC_INT).
label_comments.2B06: [RE] Type/sign test for INT-class coercion: $3DC8 type-check, error on zero/string, return sign of mantissa for integer values.
label_comments.2B12: [RE] Test FAC integer mantissa ($0CB1) for zero and return its sign via the SIGN tail.
label_comments.2B18: [RE] PUSHF: push the FAC mantissa ($0CB1) and sign/low-exp ($0CB3) onto the stack while preserving the caller return address (EX (SP),HL).
label_comments.2B25: [RE] Load operand at (HL) into regs (SUB_4EB8) then store DE/BC into the FAC; combined load-then-store helper.
label_comments.2B28: [RE] MOVFR: store DE (mantissa low) and B,C (sign/high) into the FAC cells $0CB1/$0CB3.
label_comments.2B33: [RE] MOVRF: load the FAC ($0CB1) 4-byte mantissa into E,D,C,B.
label_comments.2B36: [RE] Load 4 FP mantissa bytes from (HL) into E,D,C,B (advancing HL).
label_comments.2B38: [RE] Load 3 FP bytes from (HL) into D,C,B (entry past the first byte).
label_comments.2B3D: [RE] Tail of the (HL)->regs loaders: final INC HL and RET.
label_comments.2B3F: [RE] MOVE: copy 4 bytes from (DE) into the FAC at $0CB1.
label_comments.2B42: [RE] Block-copy 4 bytes (DE)->(HL); generic FP move primitive.
label_comments.2B47: [RE] Block-copy (DE)->(HL) of length = current value type ($0B14: 2/3/4/8 bytes); type-aware FP/string move.
label_comments.2B4B: [RE] Byte-copy loop body for the FP block moves (DJNZ over B bytes).
label_comments.2B52: [RE] Set the hidden mantissa MSB and extract the sign: force bit7 of $0CB3 high byte, save sign, return rounding bit in A.
label_comments.2B67: [RE] Load second operand from temp ($0CBA) and set up the typed-move target (SUB_4EC4_1) before a compare/op.
label_comments.2B6A: [RE] Operand setup: push the typed-move routine, type-check ($3DC8), and select the temp source ($0CAD or $0CB1) for the pending op.
label_comments.2B6F: [RE] Load second operand from temp ($0CBA) and set up the type-driven move (SUB_4EC9) before a compare/op.
label_comments.2B72: [RE] Operand setup variant selecting the typed-move (SUB_4EC9); type-check and pick temp source $0CAD/$0CB1.
label_comments.2B81: [RE] Floating-point compare (FAC vs regs operand): returns A=0 equal, $01 / $FF for greater/less from sign+exponent+mantissa comparison.
label_comments.2B9B: [RE] Mantissa-equality test: compare the 4 mantissa bytes (B,C,D,E) against (HL..); on full match pops two return levels (equal).
label_comments.2BAE: [RE] 16-bit integer compare of D:E vs H:L returning the SIGN-style -1/0/+1 result via the FP_SIGN tail.
label_comments.2BBE: [RE] Compare two FP operands (one in temp $0CBA): sign+exponent then 8-byte mantissa compare; the relational-operator comparator.
label_comments.2BC4: [RE] Body of the FP operand compare: byte-by-byte mantissa comparison ($0CC1 down) yielding the ordering result.
label_comments.2C49: [RE] Convert FAC to a 16-bit integer in HL: error if exponent>=$90 (out of range), else shift the mantissa down (FP_SHIFT_MANTISSA).
label_comments.2C58: [RE] Set the current value-type byte $0B14 to $02 (single precision).
label_comments.2C5E: [RE] Range-check helper for FP->int: compare FAC against the $8000 boundary (FCOMP) and finalize the integer result.
label_comments.2C76: [RE] CINT body: load FAC, round/scale to an integer, set the high byte and store back via FADD_NORMALIZE.
label_comments.2C89: [RE] Convert the signed 16-bit FAC integer ($0CB1) to single precision: build exponent $90 and normalize via FADD_ALIGN.
label_comments.2C8C: [RE] INT_TO_SINGLE entry with the integer already in HL: set type, form exponent $90, normalize.
label_comments.2CA2: [RE] Clear the FAC double-precision extension cells ($0CAD/$0CAF) when widening to single.
label_comments.2CAB: [RE] Set value type for a single (BC=$043E length/exp pair) and store type byte; entry used when promoting an integer to FP.
label_comments.2CB3: [RE] Type-check requiring a numeric value; error ($0D87) if string/zero — gatekeeper for an INT-class operation.
label_comments.2CDE: [RE] Decrement DE and, on borrow (DE wrapped to $FFFF), fall through to also decrement BC; multi-byte counter for the shift loop.
label_comments.2CE3: [RE] Decrement BC (low half of the shift/round counter) and return.
label_comments.2D04: [RE] FIX/round scaling: if exponent>=$98 already integral; else shift mantissa down to the integer position and re-round via FADD_COMBINE.
label_comments.2D44: [RE] Denormalize toward the integer point for FIX: set exponent $B8, align the mantissa (SUB_5306) and clear the guard cell $0CAC.
label_comments.2D6F: [RE] Borrow-decrement the FAC double-precision extension bytes from $0CAD upward (propagating the borrow through the low mantissa).
label_comments.2D96: [RE] Sign-extend HL into B (LD A,H/RLA/SBC A,A), negate via INT_NEG ($51D9), then SBC the high parts; entry into the signed-integer combine path feeding SUB_5123_1.
label_comments.2DA1: Integer ADD operator: sign-extend HL and DE, ADD HL,DE with carry into the sign byte, detect signed overflow (JP P to FP_RECOVER $4FD6); on overflow promote both operands to single via FLOAT_FROM_INT ($51F3) and re-add through FADD. MS BASIC-80 integer addition.
label_comments.2DE4: [RE] Tail of integer multiply/divide: test product sign, on overflow promote operands to float and dispatch to FMUL ($4D12) / FADD-store paths; otherwise store signed integer result via INT_NEG/FP_STORE_FAC_INT.
label_comments.2E06: [RE] Float fallback for integer mul/div overflow: if result negative store integer (FP_STORE_FAC_INT $4FD7), else convert and re-enter via SUB_500E and jump to SUB_4E76 (FAC load).
label_comments.2E14: 16-bit integer MULTIPLY via shift-and-add: $11 iterations adding BC into the running product in HL, with carry/overflow detection (Overflow error vector $0D72 on zero-operand guard). MS BASIC-80 integer multiply.
label_comments.2E4B: [RE] Combine signs of HL and DE into B, then take absolute value of HL via INT_ABS_STORE; sign-management prologue for signed integer multiply/divide.
label_comments.2E52: [RE] If HL is non-negative store it as a signed integer to the FAC (FP_STORE_FAC_INT $4FD7); otherwise fall through to INT_NEG to negate first.
label_comments.2E57: [RE] Two's-complement negate the 16-bit integer in HL (0-L, 0-H with borrow) then store to the integer FAC via FP_STORE_FAC_INT ($4FD7).
label_comments.2E61: [RE] Negate the integer FAC: load HL from $0CB1, negate via INT_NEG, return NZ unless result is the $8000 sentinel (then fall into INT_TO_SNG to promote).
label_comments.2E6C: [RE] Promote integer FAC to single-precision: move to DE, set up via SUB_502D, clear A, fall into FLOAT_FROM_INT.
label_comments.2E71: Build a single-precision FAC from the signed integer in HL: load binary exponent $98 (2^24 bias) and enter the FP normalize/pack path at SUB_4E5B. MS BASIC-80 float-from-integer.
label_comments.2E76: 16-bit integer DIVIDE: multiply-by-reciprocal then RRA-shift the quotient (D:E) into H:L with rounding, store via SUB_4FDA and INT_ABS_STORE. MS BASIC-80 integer divide.
label_comments.2E87: [RE] Flip the sign byte of the double-precision operand at $0CC0 (XOR $80), then fall into DADD (double-precision add) to perform a double subtract.
label_comments.2E8E: Double-precision (8-byte mantissa) ADD/SUBTRACT: align the operand exponent at $0CC1 against the accumulator exponent $0CB4, denormalize-shift, add/subtract the 8-byte mantissas at $0CAC vs $0CBA, renormalize. Core of MBF double-precision addition.
label_comments.2F3F: [RE] Round-up / carry-propagate the 8-byte double mantissa (INC through $0CAD..; on full carry set MSB $80 and bump exponent), called when the high mantissa byte is negative after add.
label_comments.2F52: [RE] Subtract constant $8E from accumulator and add the 7-byte block $0CBA into $0CAD; thin wrapper that presets A and falls into the multi-byte add loop DP_ADD_BLOCK.
label_comments.2F5B: [RE] Preset constant $8E (LD A,$8E) for the multi-byte mantissa add, then fall into DP_ADD_BLOCK; used by the double multiply/divide inner loop.
label_comments.2F5D: [RE] Set up source $0CBA and dest $0CAD for a 7-byte chained ADC, store the constant operand byte into the loop, then run DP_ADD_BLOCK.
label_comments.2F60: [RE] 7-byte chained add-with-carry loop: ADC each byte of [HL]=$0CBA into [DE]=$0CAD, advancing both pointers. Multi-byte mantissa addition primitive for double-precision arithmetic.
label_comments.2F72: [RE] Two's-complement negate the 8-byte double mantissa starting at $0CAC (CPL the guard byte, then chained 0-byte SBC across 8 bytes).
label_comments.2F84: [RE] Denormalize: shift the 8-byte double mantissa right by N bit-positions (in groups of 8 via byte moves, remainder by per-bit RRA) to align exponents before DADD.
label_comments.2F8B: [RE] Inner byte/bit right-shift loop for DP_SHIFT_RIGHT_N: moves whole bytes while the shift count exceeds 8, then performs the leftover bit shifts.
label_comments.2FAB: [RE] Right-shift the double mantissa by 1 starting at $0CB3 (sets D=$01), used between partial products in the double multiply.
label_comments.2FB2: [RE] Shift the 8-byte double mantissa left by one bit (chained RLA across 8 bytes from [HL]); partial-product accumulation step for double multiply.
label_comments.2FBC: Double-precision MULTIPLY: for each of 7 mantissa bytes / 8 bits, conditionally add (DP_ADD_CONST) the multiplicand and shift, accumulating the 8-byte product; guards against multiply-by-zero. MS BASIC-80 double multiply.
label_comments.2FF6: [RE] Double-precision MBF constant (8-byte mantissa $CD CC CC CC CC CC 4C exp $7D ~= 0.1) used as the divide-by-ten reciprocal seed by DDIV/the decimal scaler.
label_comments.3002: [RE] Small double-precision MBF constant ($00 $00 $20 $84) following DP_CONST_TENTH, used as a rounding/scaling constant by the double-precision path.
label_comments.3006: Double-precision DIVIDE: handle exponent/sign, restoring-division by 15 iterations (DP_PUSH_OPERAND / DADD / DP_POP_OPERAND), building the quotient mantissa; promotes to single (SUB_4E76) when exponent small. MS BASIC-80 double divide.
label_comments.3066: [RE] Decrement the double-precision exponent at $0CB4; on underflow to zero jump to FP_ZERO ($4C09). Renormalization step for DMUL/DDIV.
label_comments.306E: [RE] Decrement the operand exponent byte $0CC1 by up to 4, returning early when it reaches zero; quad-loop counter for the divide reciprocal expansion.
label_comments.307A: [RE] Push the 4 word-pairs of the 8-byte double operand at $0CBA onto the stack (saving the return address), preserving it across an inner DADD in DDIV.
label_comments.308B: [RE] Restore the 8-byte double operand into $0CC1..$0CBA from the stack (inverse of DP_PUSH_OPERAND).
label_comments.309C: [RE] Double-precision FIX/INT and decimal-scale helper: validates exponents, copies FAC to temp via DP_COPY_TEMP ($5474), then repeatedly subtracts powers of ten ($9E/$8E constants) to extract integer digits.
label_comments.30F2: [RE] Copy the 7-byte double mantissa down into temp buffer $0CE3 (and clear the source), saving the leading byte to $0CC0; scratch save used by DMUL/DDIV/DP_FIX.
label_comments.3106: [RE] Multiply the double accumulator by ten: bump the mantissa length, add the value to itself shifted (DADD), used by the decimal input/output scaler. Double-precision *10 step.
label_comments.311E: FIN: parse an ASCII numeric literal into the FAC. Handles leading sign, decimal point, E/D exponent markers, and type suffixes (%=int, !=sng, #=dbl, $=str); accumulates digits with integer->single->double promotion. MS BASIC-80 string-to-number.
label_comments.31F3: [RE] FIN coercion: force the parsed value to integer (FN_CINT) when Z, else to single (FN_CSNG) when NZ, preserving all registers.
label_comments.3202: [RE] FIN accumulate digit *10: RET if count zero, else multiply the running value by ten using the single-precision (SUB_4E30) or double-precision (DP_MUL10 $5488) path per type parity; returns A decremented.
label_comments.3203: [RE] Body of FIN_MUL10 (entry past the RET Z guard): performs the *10 multiply by single- or double-precision path.
label_comments.3212: [RE] FIN fractional scale-down: divide the running value by ten via the single-precision (SUB_4D6A) or double-precision (DDIV $5388) path per type parity; returns A incremented to track the decimal exponent.
label_comments.3225: [RE] FIN integer-accumulate a decimal digit into $0CB1 (HL = HL*10 + digit) while the value still fits 16 bits; on overflow it promotes to single/double (constant $9474/$2400) and continues in float.
label_comments.3289: [RE] FIN finalize: store the completed FAC (SUB_4E9A / SUB_4E56), unwind the saved registers and dispatch through SUB_4BA6 to return the converted number to the expression evaluator.
label_comments.3294: [RE] FIN exponent-field digit accumulate: E*10+digit into E with range guard (<10), used while reading the E/D exponent of a numeric literal; errors via $0D81 path on overflow.
label_comments.3389: [RE] Print the FOUT-formatted ASCII number string from buffer $0CED via STROUT ($6C40), preserving HL; the console-output wrapper used after FOUT builds the digit string.
label_comments.33A0: [RE] MS BASIC-80 FOUT: convert FAC (mantissa $0CB1 / exp $0CB4 / type $0B14) to a decimal/exponential ASCII string in the $0CC3 buffer, A=0 -> no format flags. Returns HL -> string. Used by STR$/PRINT/LIST.
label_comments.33A1: [RE] FOUT with PRINT USING format flags in A (stored to $0B4A via FOUT_SET_FORMAT); handles sign placement then dispatches by value type.
label_comments.33D8: [RE] PRINT USING numeric-field scanner: walks the format-image field at $0CC3 ('#', '.', ',', '$', '*', '+', 'E/D' exponent), records width/flags, and rewrites fill characters (space/'*'/'$').
label_comments.341F: [RE] Store the PRINT USING format byte (A) to $0B4A and reset the output buffer head ($0CC3) to a leading space.
label_comments.3428: [RE] FIN core (ASCII -> floating): scans the digit/decimal/exponent text, counts integer+fraction digits, parses the E/D exponent ($CB8 = exp-overflow flag), and converts via the decimal-accumulate path (CALLs FOUT_DIGITS_* / power-of-ten scaling). Returns the value in FAC.
label_comments.34D3: [RE] FOUT decimal core: rounds the FAC to N significant digits, computes the base-10 exponent, generates the digit string and trims trailing zeros; decides fixed vs E-notation.
label_comments.3518: [RE] Append the exponent suffix (E/D, sign, two decimal digits) to the formatted number and NUL-terminate; also the PRINT USING field-assembly path that interleaves the digit string with the format image (commas, '$', '*', '+'/'-').
label_comments.36BA: [RE] Scale FAC into the [1,10) digit-generation range by repeated multiply/divide by powers of ten (tables at $5BC0/$5BC8/$5BD0), tracking the decimal exponent in the saved counter.
label_comments.370D: [RE] One power-of-ten comparison/normalize step for FOUT_SCALE10: compares FAC against 10 / 1e-? bounds and multiplies or divides as needed.
label_comments.3729: [RE] Emit A leading/trailing '0' digits into the output buffer (HL), decrementing A to zero.
label_comments.3731: [RE] Emit A '0' digits, inserting the decimal point / comma separators via FOUT_DIGIT_SEP as the digit position requires (PRINT USING fractional fill).
label_comments.3737: [RE] Inner loop: store a '0' digit, advance, decrement count (shared tail of FOUT_EMIT_ZEROS_DP).
label_comments.373D: [RE] Compute the comma-group digit counter (C) and total digit width (B) for a PRINT USING numeric field from the integer/fraction digit counts in D/E.
label_comments.3749: [RE] Test the PRINT USING comma-grouping flag (bit 6 of format byte $0B4A); returns with C set/cleared to enable thousands separators.
label_comments.3751: [RE] Emit the decimal point plus leading-zero run for a pure-fraction value (|x|<1): records the decimal-point position at $0B69 and fills '0' digits.
label_comments.3764: [RE] Per-digit separator emitter: drops the decimal point ('.', records pos at $0B69) when the integer digits are exhausted, or a comma every 3 digits when grouping is enabled.
label_comments.3767: [RE] Emit the decimal point '.', record its buffer position at $0B69, and (when grouping) reseed the comma counter to 3.
label_comments.3771: [RE] Emit a thousands-separator comma ',' and reset the 3-digit group counter.
label_comments.3777: [RE] Generate fractional decimal digits: repeatedly multiply the FAC fraction by 10 (via the BCD constant tables $5BD8/$5BE8/$5C2E) and emit each integer digit through FOUT_DIGIT_SEP.
label_comments.3809: [RE] Generate integer decimal digits by repeated subtraction of the power-of-ten table at $5C34 (10000/1000/100/10/1) from the FAC mantissa $0CB1, emitting each via FOUT_DIGIT_SEP.
label_comments.38BC: [RE] HEX$/OCT$ and LIST &H/&O converter: turns the 16-bit integer in HL into a hex (4 nibbles) or octal (6 groups) ASCII string at $0CC2; B selects base (0=octal width, hex entry at $5C41). Dispatched from the LIST detokenizer ($41FD/$4202).
label_comments.3906: [RE] Helper that pushes the FAC-negate routine ($4E76) as a return address then JP (HL); used to conditionally negate the FAC in the transcendental handlers.
label_comments.39D4: [RE] Odd-power polynomial evaluator: forms x*P(x^2) for the series approximations (SIN/ATN/TAN), squaring the argument then calling the Horner evaluator POLY_EVAL.
label_comments.39E3: [RE] MS BASIC-80 polynomial (Horner) evaluator: HL -> coefficient table (count byte then MBF coefficients); repeatedly FMUL by the argument and FADD the next coefficient. Shared by LOG/EXP/SIN/COS/TAN/ATN.
label_comments.3A0A: [RE] Evaluate a polynomial in sqrt(x): pushes the argument, runs FN_SQR, then evaluates the series via POLY_EVAL (used by the ATN/LOG support path called at $4486).
label_comments.3BAE: [RE] PTRGET front-end: scan a variable name at the BASIC text pointer (HL). Accumulates the leading alpha + following alphanumerics (high-bit set) into the VARNAM buffer $0871, honouring type-suffix chars %/$/!/# to set VALTYP $0B14 (and the default-type table at $0B36), then falls into the table search at PTRGET_SEARCH ($5FC9). Called by LET ($5F35) and FRMEVL operand fetch.
label_comments.3C47: [RE] PTRGET search/allocate core: walk the simple-variable table ($0B6F..$0B71) for the packed name in C/$0B14/$0871; on a hit return its address, on a miss create a new entry (allocate via STR/var-space grow, SUB_6811/SUB_622E). Detects '(' to branch to the array path (subscript eval + array search/alloc, $612C loop) honouring DIM and OPTION BASE.
label_comments.3EA3: [RE] Advance HL past one variable/array-table entry: load length byte at (HL), then add it to HL (falls into VARTAB_ADD_LEN). Used while scanning the array table during PTRGET and by the array-walk in DIM/index code ($6CBF, $740E...).
label_comments.3EA5: [RE] HL += A (zero-extended) preserving BC: B:=0, C:=A, ADD HL,BC. Entry point used to step over a table entry whose length is already in A.
label_comments.3EAC: [RE] Copy the scanned variable name from the VARNAM buffer $0871 (length-prefixed) into the new variable-table entry at (HL), advancing HL. Used by PTRGET_SEARCH when creating a fresh entry.
label_comments.3EC0: [RE] Compare the variable name held at (HL) in a table entry against the scanned name in buffer $0872, length A. Returns Z on full match; on mismatch advances HL past the rest of the name (via VARTAB_ADD_LEN) and returns NZ. Drives the linear scan in PTRGET_SEARCH.
label_comments.3EDB: [RE] EDIT-statement line-number resolver: store the LIST/edit flag at $0835, fetch the current/target line pointer from $0B60, and fall into the EDIT statement handler (STMT_EDIT) to enter the line editor.
label_comments.3FA4: [RE] Line-editor helper: echo D characters of the line buffer at (HL) to the console (OUTCHR via SUB_6800), advancing HL and the column count B; ESC ($1B) enters the copy/scan sub-mode (EDIT_COPY_MODE). Part of the EDIT/line-input screen editor.
label_comments.3FF9: [RE] Line-editor helper: emit a '\' ($5C) line-terminator marker through OUTCHR; entry SUB_637B_1/_2 read replacement characters from the console (CONIN), filtering control chars, and overwrite the buffer (insert/overtype) for the EDIT screen editor.
label_comments.4068: [RE] Line-editor buffer-shift helper: pull characters down within the edit buffer at (HL) until the NUL terminator (used for delete/backspace in the EDIT screen editor). The continuation labels SUB_63EA_9.. share code into the PRINT USING formatter below.
label_comments.40D6: [RE] PRINT USING statement engine (reached from the PRINT dispatcher on token $E8 'USING'): evaluate the format string, then scan its field characters - '#' digit positions, '.' decimal point, ',' grouping, '+'/'-' sign, '$$' float-dollar, '**' asterisk-fill, '^^^^' exponential, '\ \' string field, '!' first-char, '&' variable string - formatting each argument (FOUT/SUB_5723 for numbers, STROUT for strings) and looping over the value list.
label_comments.4287: [RE] PRINT USING helper: if the pending-sign flag in D is set, emit a leading '+' ($2B) via OUTCHR before the formatted field; preserves AF.
label_comments.42EA: [RE] Low-level BIOS console-out vector wrapper: char in A, saves BC/DE/HL, copies to C, CALLs the runtime-patched $0000 cell (CP/M BIOS CONOUT, installed by cold start at SUB_666C_1+1 / $8217). The device-output primitive that OUTCHR ($6613) and OUTDO_WIDTH ($6682) route through.
label_comments.42F7: [RE] Clears the output-suppress flag ($0838) and, if the print column ($0837) is nonzero, falls through to OUTDO_WIDTH to emit CRLF and reset the column. The 'return to start of line' helper before fresh output.
label_comments.4382: [RE] Second BIOS console-out vector wrapper (raw byte emit): char in A->C, saves regs, CALLs the runtime-patched $0000 cell (separate CP/M BIOS output entry installed by cold start at SUB_6704_1+1 / $820D). Used by OUTDO_WIDTH for the actual character emission distinct from OUTDO_DEVICE.
label_comments.438F: [RE] Force end-of-line bookkeeping: calls SUB_6792 to clear column state, then falls into LINE_COUNT_INC to bump the printed-line counter / page check ($0B12 vs $083C).
label_comments.4392: [RE] Increments the printed-line counter ($0B12) up to the page limit ($083C); clamps at the limit and returns A=0. Drives the auto-page / line-width newline logic in OUTDO_WIDTH.
label_comments.43A2: [RE] Auto-page 'more' handler on the LIST/output path: skips when output is redirected ($0840 nonzero), tests pending key (SUB_7C0B), runs the input poll (SUB_781A) and Ctrl-C/break check ($0CA0), and on a full page pushes STMT_FOR_7 and prints the pause prompt string at $0CF2 via STROUT before resuming.
label_comments.43F9: [RE] If the auto-print column flag ($0B11) is nonzero, emit CR/LF (CRLF $6788); else return. Ensures output starts on a fresh line.
label_comments.4410: [RE] Clear pending auto-line / print-column state ($0837 column, $0838, $0B11) after a newline; consulted via $0840 line-input-in-progress guard.
label_comments.4472: [RE] Fetch and clear the pending-key cell ($0834) set by INKEY_SCAN; returns Z if no key pending, else the key in A with the cell zeroed.
label_comments.447E: [RE] Print a char via OUTCHR ($6613); if it was LF ($0A) also emit CR ($0D) and reset print state. Newline-expanding console write.
label_comments.448F: [RE] After GC_CHECK_AND_COLLECT, copy a string of BC bytes downward from (HL) to (BC dest), comparing via HL/DE-compare; the string-move primitive used by string assignment.
label_comments.4492: [RE] String copy loop without the prior heap check: move bytes from (HL) to (BC) decrementing both until HL==DE (SUB_691F compare). Tail of STR_COPY_DOWN.
label_comments.44D5: [RE] Compare a candidate string-heap address (HL) against the string/var-space top pointer ($0B48) via the 16-bit compare; returns carry if the allocation would collide. Used by GC_CHECK_AND_COLLECT.
label_comments.450B: [RE] CLEAR/RUN reinit: load program start ($0846), step back, fall into the storage-map reset that re-points the variable/array/string base pointers ($0B54 etc.).
label_comments.450F: [RE] Core CLEAR/NEW/RUN storage re-initialization: rebuilds the variable, array, FOR/file-slot, DATA and string-heap pointers, clears the math accumulator slots, resets stack and graphics state; common tail of NEW/CLEAR/RUN.
label_comments.459D: MS BASIC 16-bit compare: A=H-D then (if equal) A=L-E, setting Z when HL==DE and carry per HL<DE. The pervasive pointer-compare primitive (68 call sites).
label_comments.460E: [RE] Entry with A=$0F: echo a Ctrl-O as '^O'; falls into ECHO_CTRL_CHAR to print '^' + (ctrl+$40) then CRLF.
label_comments.4610: [RE] Echo a control character as caret notation: prints '^' ($5E) then the char+$40 via OUTCHR, then CRLF; on Ctrl-C ($03) also clears the pause/suppress flags ($0838/$083F).
label_comments.46BE: [RE] Test (HL): set carry if the char is a letter A-Z ($41-$5A); returns carry/no-carry to classify identifier start chars.
label_comments.46BF: [RE] Same letter test on the char already in A: carry if A in $41-$5A, else clear (CCF after the upper-bound test).
label_comments.4749: [RE] 16-bit subtract: DE = HL - DE (LD A,L/SUB E / LD A,H/SBC D), used by CLEAR to size the protected memory region.
label_comments.475A: [RE] Core of the NEXT statement: locate the matching FOR frame on the stack ($0CFD search), apply the STEP, compare against the limit, and either re-enter the loop (STMT_FOR_6) or fall through to loop exit; also handles 'NEXT var,var'.
label_comments.4837: [RE] Finalize a string-returning function result: scan the formed string (SUB_6BEA), build its descriptor and stage it as the string temporary returned through SUB_6E0B.
label_comments.4844: [RE] Build/allocate a string body from a descriptor: for the byte at (HL) call GETSPA to reserve space, copy via SUB_6DB0, returning the new descriptor; used by string concatenation/formatting.
label_comments.4858: [RE] Allocate a 1-byte string (A=1) then build its descriptor; convenience entry into the descriptor builder for single-char string results (e.g. CHR$, INKEY$).
label_comments.485A: [RE] Allocate an A-byte string via GETSPA, then fall into STORE_STR_DESC to record length/pointer.
label_comments.485D: [RE] Store a string descriptor (length A, body pointer DE) into the temporary descriptor cell $0B45..$0B47 and return HL pointing at it.
label_comments.4868: [RE] Scan a quoted/argument string starting before (HL): default terminator '"' ($22); measures length to the closing quote/NUL and forms a descriptor. PRINT/string-constant scanner (call sites in CRUNCH/FRMEVL/PRINT).
label_comments.4869: [RE] Entry with B set to the open-quote terminator $22; sets D=B and scans the string body.
label_comments.486B: [RE] Entry with the terminator byte preset in B; copies to D and scans to that terminator or NUL.
label_comments.486C: [RE] String-scan inner loop: walk (HL) counting chars in C until a NUL, the B-terminator or D-terminator; trims trailing spaces after a comma terminator and stores the resulting descriptor (length C).
label_comments.4898: [RE] Place a string descriptor into the rotating string-temporary table (pointer $0B25, base $0B48): records type=string ($0B14=3), stores the descriptor and advances the temp pointer; on overflow raises 'String formula too complex' (E=$10 via $0D89). Widely used to stage string FRMEVL results.
label_comments.49A6: [RE] GARBAG helper: scan a string descriptor (length+ptr at HL) and, if the string lives below the current collection watermark ($0B48), record it as the new candidate highest free-able block; advances HL past the descriptor.
label_comments.49EE: [RE] FRESTR/movestring helper: pull a string's descriptor (via FRMEVL_EVAL_OPERAND), free its data with SUB_6DC0, then copy the bytes into freshly-allocated string space (SUB_6DA8 block copies); returns through FRMEVL fixup.
label_comments.4A26: [RE] copy one string-descriptor's data: pops the descriptor (len in A, addr in BC) off the caller's stack and block-copies len bytes from (BC) to (DE) via SUB_6DB0.
label_comments.4A2E: [RE] copy A (=L) bytes from (BC) to (DE), ascending; INC L / DEC L sets the counter, RET Z when done. Generic ascending memory move.
label_comments.4A37: MS BASIC-80 FRETMP: free the most-recent temporary string descriptor (CALL FREFAC at $5035), then fall into FRESTR to reclaim its heap bytes if it was the topmost allocation.
label_comments.4A3A: MS BASIC-80 FRESTR: free the string whose descriptor pointer is in FAC ($0CB1); loads the descriptor then frees its data via FRESTR1 (SUB_6DC0).
label_comments.4A3D: MS BASIC-80 FRESTR entry with descriptor address already in HL: EX DE,HL then free.
label_comments.4A3E: MS BASIC-80 FRESTR1: if the descriptor points at the topmost string-heap allocation, hand its bytes back by advancing the top-of-free-string pointer ($0B48); otherwise leave the heap unchanged.
label_comments.4A57: [RE] pop the most-recently-pushed temporary string descriptor off the temp-descriptor stack ($0B25): if HL matches, retract the temp pointer by one descriptor and clear Z.
label_comments.4A6B: [RE] evaluate the pending string argument to a descriptor (via FRETMP), returning the descriptor's length in A and address in HL; Z set if the string is empty.
label_comments.4A77: [RE] VAL() body: fetch the string descriptor, error if empty, then load the string's text pointer (DE) and first byte (A) for numeric parsing (FIN).
label_comments.4A89: [RE] string-function epilogue: store result char (E) into the string work buffer at ($0B46) and return through SUB_6C1A (FRMEVL string-temp fixup).
label_comments.4A91: [RE] MID$() function body (token $E7): parse '(' source$ ',' start [ ',' len ] ')', then drop into the LEFT$/RIGHT$/MID$ common copy path.
label_comments.4ABF: [RE] STRING$/SPACE$ build helper: allocate B bytes of string space, fill with the pad char in A, returning the new descriptor; entry from the function epilogue at SUB_6E0B.
label_comments.4ACD: [RE] fill loop: write fill char A into B successive bytes of the freshly allocated string buffer at ($0B46).
label_comments.4AD3: [RE] LEFT$/RIGHT$/MID$ common tail: parse the length byte, clamp it to the source length, allocate a new string of that size and copy the selected substring into it; returns via FRMEVL string-temp fixup.
label_comments.4B2D: [RE] string-function helper (reached via the function dispatch table): fetch the string descriptor, index to byte E within it, read/replace that byte and re-tokenise (used by a single-char string accessor).
label_comments.4B4A: [RE] parse a required numeric byte argument terminated by ')': SYNCHR ')' then return the prior length byte in B from the caller's pushed args.
label_comments.4B4F: [RE] recover the source-string length byte into B from the two stacked descriptor halves, preserving the return address.
label_comments.4B54: [RE] INSTR() body (token $E9): parse optional start position then the two string arguments, search for the second string inside the first and return the 1-based match index (0 if not found).
label_comments.4BAD: [RE] INSTR scan: at each candidate offset compare the needle against the haystack byte-by-byte; on full match return its 1-based position, else advance one character.
label_comments.4BE1: [RE] LET MID$(var$,start[,len])=src$ assignment body: locate the target string in place (allocating/copying it down out of program/heap space if needed), then overwrite the selected character range with the source string's bytes (no length change).
label_comments.4C5F: [RE] parse an optional second/length argument: default $FF (whole string) when next char is ')', otherwise SYNCHR ',' and read a byte expression; ends by checking for ')'.
label_comments.4C87: MS BASIC-80 QINLIN: print the '? ' input prompt then fall into the console line-input editor (INLIN). Called for INPUT and for the RANDOMIZE seed prompt.
label_comments.4C94: [RE] INLIN per-character fetch: read one console key (CONIN at $6724); Ctrl-A ($01) toggles into line-edit/redisplay, otherwise dispatch the character in the editor.
label_comments.4CA0: [RE] store char then reset editor state: clear the pending-control and auto-quote flags ($0834/$0C93) at the start of a fresh input line.
label_comments.4CA9: MS BASIC-80 INLIN: console line-input editor main loop. Reads keys, echoes printable characters into the line buffer at $0A0E, and handles control keys (CR, BS/Ctrl-H, Ctrl-U, Ctrl-R, Ctrl-X, Tab, LF, DEL) building an edited line; returns it with CY=Ctrl-C abort.
label_comments.4CBC: [RE] DEL/rubout handling: echo a backslash on first delete, then erase one character from the buffer, updating the echo state at $083E.
label_comments.4CD8: [RE] continue rubout: emit the deleted character (in backslash-echo mode) and loop while more remain.
label_comments.4CDF: [RE] Ctrl-U / line-kill finish: echo the trailing char, CRLF, reset the prompt buffer pointer ($0A0E) and start the line over.
label_comments.4CF0: [RE] INLIN control-character dispatch: classify the input char (Bell $07, CR $0D, Tab $09, LF $0A, Ctrl-U $15, BS $08, Ctrl-X $18, Ctrl-R $12, < $20 ignore) and branch; printable chars fall through to the store path.
label_comments.4D3A: [RE] Ctrl-X handling: discard the current line by echoing '#' and restarting the editor (jumps to the line-kill finish).
label_comments.4D44: [RE] Ctrl-R / retype-line: terminate the buffer, CRLF, redisplay the line accumulated so far (SUB_4100) from $0A0E, then resume editing.
label_comments.4D61: [RE] store a printable character: guard against buffer overflow (255 chars -> Bell and reformat via $34E0/CRUNCH_EMIT), else append to the buffer, echo it, and detect end-of-line on LF.
label_comments.4D7D: [RE] append the accepted char C to the buffer, bump the count, echo it; on a literal newline reset the print column ($0B11) and wait for the continuation key.
label_comments.4D91: [RE] after echoing a hard LF, poll the console until a non-null key arrives; CR ends the line, anything else re-enters the dispatcher.
label_comments.4D9F: [RE] CR / end-of-line: if in auto-quote ('?') redisplay mode return the editor's prefilled buffer ($0A0D); otherwise terminate the buffer and return the completed line.
label_comments.4DAC: [RE] INPUT/LINE-INPUT prompt-separator: clear the auto-prompt flag ($0C93); if the next char is ';' set the flag (suppress the trailing '?') and CHRGET past it.
label_comments.4DBC: [RE] snapshot the current print column ($0B11) into the Tab-expansion base cell (self-modified operand at $719D) so Tab stops align to where the prompt left the cursor.
label_comments.4DC3: [RE] backspace/Ctrl-H handling: if erasing over a LF redisplay the line; if over a Tab recompute and back up the right number of columns to the previous tab stop; else erase one echoed character.
label_comments.4DD0: [RE] re-echo the buffered line characters from HL for B bytes (used when backspacing past a newline).
label_comments.4E06: [RE] erase A character cells on the console by emitting BS/space/BS B times (visual rubout of B columns).
label_comments.4E80: [RE] WHILE/WEND helper: walk the runtime stack frames (skipping FOR entries, marker $82) looking for a matching WHILE frame (marker $AF) whose loop-text pointer matches; returns the frame in HL with Z set on match.
label_comments.4EA8: [RE] WEND without matching WHILE: raise coded error $1E ('WEND without WHILE') via the error dispatcher at $0D89.
label_comments.503E: [RE] CHAIN-ALL/COMMON helper: set the high bit (preserve flag) on a named variable so the post-CHAIN cleanup keeps it across the program reload.
label_comments.50A6: [RE] CHAIN preserve pass: relocate kept simple/array variables, copying their bytes up out of the way (via SUB_691F compare and block move) and adjusting the variable/array area pointers ($0B71/$0B73) so they survive the new program load.
