label_comments.50C1: [RE] CHAIN preserve pass over arrays: walk the array table, temporarily clearing each entry's preserve bit, summing sizes (SUB_6225) and relocating the kept ones.
label_comments.50E9: [RE] CHAIN preserve pass over string variables/arrays: walk simple-variable and array storage ($0B6F/$0B71), freeing/relocating string descriptors (type 3) of kept variables via SUB_74C5.
label_comments.5143: [RE] move one kept string's data during CHAIN: if the string lives in program/heap space that the reload will clobber, copy it up into safe string space (SUB_6BC6/SUB_4ECD) and rewrite its descriptor pointer.
label_comments.516D: [RE] CHAIN string-space compaction: garbage-collect, then slide the preserved string heap (between $0B6F/$0B71/$0B73) to its new base, recording the move delta for descriptor fixups.
label_comments.528B: [RE] Entry for routines needing a default file# of 1: sets C=1 then falls into PARSE_FILENUM_HASH.
label_comments.528D: [RE] If next char is '#', skip it and parse the file-number expr via FILE_NUM_TO_FCB; else return with file# defaulted (C). Used by PRINT#/INPUT#/WRITE#/GET/PUT.
label_comments.52A1: [RE] Set current-file FCB pointer (PTRFIL $0840) = BC (the resolved file number); HL preserved.
label_comments.52A9: [RE] CHRGET past optional '#', FRMEVL the file-number expr, range-check vs max open files ($0870); index file table at $0850 to BC=FCB base, return mode byte in A ('File not OPEN' if 0).
label_comments.52B5: [RE] FILE_NUM_TO_FCB wrapper that sets Z per the FCB mode byte (caller checks open/closed).
label_comments.52B8: [RE] Resolve file# then return the FCB mode byte (file-open type) in A via the table lookup at 763B.
label_comments.52B9: [RE] Given file# in E, index file-table $0850 to BC=FCB, load mode byte A=(FCB[0]); OR A sets flags.
label_comments.52CF: [RE] Return DE = pointer to the file's data buffer inside its FCB: offset $29 for sequential, $B2 for random (per mode byte).
label_comments.53EE: [RE] INPUT#/LINE INPUT# helper: store char A into input buffer (HL++), decrement field count B; on underflow pop caller and bail.
label_comments.53F7: [RE] LOAD entry: D=1 (open-for-input mode) then fall into the file-open core.
label_comments.53F9: [RE] LOAD/SAVE file-open stub: select channel #0 (XOR A) and enter the OPEN core with the access mode in D.
label_comments.5498: [RE] After LOAD/RUN: close all open files (via CLOSE-all) and re-init variable space (CLEAR_VARS).
label_comments.54A1: [RE] RUN with no filename: CLEAR_VARS then jump into the program-run / stack-reset path.
label_comments.5503: [RE] SAVE core: write the tokenized program image (line links + text) byte-by-byte to the open output file via PUTC_FILE (SUB_7B22), terminating at end-of-program.
label_comments.552D: [RE] CLOSE-all loop trampoline (executable bytes shown as DEFB): POP regs, decrement file index, loop to close next file, else POP HL/RET.
label_comments.5535: [RE] CLOSE continuation trampoline (executable DEFB): after closing one file, restore BC and check for ',' to close the next listed file number.
label_comments.554F: [RE] Close every open file (A=0 -> CLOSE-all path of STMT_CLOSE); preserves BC/DE. Used by RUN/NEW/SYSTEM/RESET.
label_comments.5645: [RE] LSET/RSET helper: pad the field buffer (DE) with C spaces; used to blank/justify the fixed-width field.
label_comments.56FF: [RE] Flush the current record via BDOS Write-Sequential ($15) (read-seq = $14); function code from $08CD.
label_comments.5702: [RE] Write-sequential error check: BDOS Write-Sequential = $15 (function code held at $08CD).
label_comments.573C: [RE] Close a single open file: write trailing Ctrl-Z/EOF + flush partial record if dirty, BDOS Close-File ($10), then zero the FCB slot.
label_comments.5779: [RE] LOC() function body: return current record/position number from the FCB (random offset $AE vs sequential $26).
label_comments.5790: [RE] LOF()/file-size helper: read a length byte from the FCB ($10 field) and return it as a numeric value.
label_comments.57A0: [RE] Write one byte (A) to the open sequential file buffer; when the 128-byte record fills, flush it via FILE_FLUSH_RECORD and advance the record number.
label_comments.5808: [RE] LDIR copy of a 128-byte (one CP/M record) block; BC preserved.
label_comments.5810: [RE] Read next byte from the open sequential input file buffer; when the buffer is exhausted, refill it with the next record (sets carry/Ctrl-Z at EOF).
label_comments.583F: [RE] Read the next sequential record from the current file into its FCB buffer (entry that loads $0840 first).
label_comments.5842: [RE] Bump the FCB record number and BDOS Read-Sequential into the buffer; sets the buffer-status byte (0=data, $80=EOF).
label_comments.5878: [RE] Point CP/M DMA at this file's 128-byte buffer (FCB+$28) via BDOS Set-DMA ($1A) before a read/write.
label_comments.5889: [RE] GETC_FILE wrapper that detects Ctrl-Z ($1A) as end-of-file: marks the FCB EOF fields and returns carry on EOF.
label_comments.58A4: [RE] FRMEVL a string filename, then parse drive/name/ext into the scratch CP/M FCB at $08AA (uppercased, space-padded fields).
label_comments.58FE: [RE] Pad the remaining name/extension field bytes of the FCB with spaces ($20) to the fixed width.
label_comments.5A87: [RE] KILL statement: parse filename into the default FCB ($0080/$08AA) then BDOS delete-file (C=$13); '?' from BDOS -> error $0D4A. Reached via dispatch DEFW SUB_7E09 at $7A58.
label_comments.5AB1: [RE] FILES statement: directory listing. Optional filespec parsed into FCB ($08AA), '*' expanded to '?' (SUB_7EBB), BDOS set-DMA ($1A) + search-first ($11)/search-next ($12); prints each 11-char name with '.' between name and extension via OUTCHR, columns from print-col ($083B).
label_comments.5B39: [RE] If FCB byte is '*' fall into FCB_WILD_EXPAND, else return: handles the leading-'*' wildcard in a FILES/KILL filespec.
label_comments.5B3D: [RE] Fill C FCB name/ext bytes with '?' ($3F) to turn a '*' wildcard into an all-match field for BDOS directory search.
label_comments.5B44: [RE] BDOS file-op wrapper: issue BDOS function (A->C) with DE=FCB, then bump the random-record/overflow counter at FCB+$21..$23; map BDOS A-result to BASIC code (0=ok/RET Z, 5=dir-full err $0D62, else 1/2). Used by OPEN/CLOSE/random GET-PUT.
label_comments.5B70: [RE] Sequential file read into the data buffer: set DMA via FCB cursor, loop BDOS read-sequential ($14) of 128-byte records (SUB_7F25 sets FCB ptr), copying each record into the user buffer until DE count exhausted.
label_comments.5BA3: [RE] Compute the FCB working pointer (current file-table entry $0B48 - $2A*1 reverse offset) and verify stack room (SUB_691F/SUB_781A); used before each BDOS sequential transfer.
label_comments.5BB1: [RE] Range-check the file number against the open-file table limit ($0C97) via DCOMPR.
label_comments.5CF4: [RE] Random-file PUT inner helper: walk the FIELD descriptor chain (FCB+$AB pointer pair), advance the write cursor (SUB_81AC_1), and on buffer-full dispatch the record write (SUB_7B22_3 at $7B5D). Entered at $8077 (skip the OR $AF flag-set) for the no-flag variant.
label_comments.5D2A: [RE] Copy BC bytes (HL)->(DE) for FIELD/GET/PUT record buffering; preserves BC.
label_comments.5D78: [RE] Load the FIELD buffer base pointer pair (FCB+$A9) into DE.
label_comments.5D7D: [RE] Load the FIELD current-position pointer pair (FCB+$B0) into DE.
label_comments.5D85: [RE] Advance (INC DE) and store the FIELD current-position pointer (FCB+$B0).
label_comments.5D8E: [RE] Set BC=FCB then compute remaining bytes in the FIELD buffer: position ptr (FCB+$B0) vs buffer base (FCB+$A9); Z when buffer exhausted (EOF/refill needed).
label_comments.5D90: [RE] Remaining-bytes-in-buffer test with BC already = FCB base (FCB+$B0 position vs FCB+$A9 base); returns Z if empty.
label_comments.5DB4: [RE] MS BASIC-80 protected-program DECODE: XOR each program byte ($0846..$0B6F) against two rotating key tables (period 13 from FN_TAN_2, period 11 from FN_RND_5) plus rotating additive constants -- the 'saved with ,P' obfuscation reversal.
label_comments.5DEB: [RE] MS BASIC-80 protected-program ENCODE: inverse of PROG_UNSCRAMBLE, re-applies the dual rotating-key XOR over the program area so the in-memory image stays protected.
label_comments.5E21: [RE] RET unless SAVTXT ($0844) == $FFFF, in which case fall into the function-call guard.
label_comments.5E2A: [RE] Illegal-direct guard: if the 'running a program' flag ($0C99) is clear -> $0D5C (Illegal direct), else RET preserving AF. Statements that need a stored line call here.
label_comments.0858: [RE] Active-error/RESUME state flag (MS BASIC ERRFLG): ON-ERROR loads it as the in-effect error code E ($3669); RESUME stores INC'd value ($3695); cold-start zeroes it ($826E). Same byte reused as the LIST/EDIT mode flag by the EDIT line resolver ($625D).
label_comments.0862: Ctrl-O output-suppress toggle: nonzero discards console output. Cleared at READY/cold-start, toggled by CONIN on Ctrl-O ($0F) and cleared on Ctrl-C. (Already commented in file.)
label_comments.0863: Active-file FCB pointer (MS BASIC PTRFIL): 0 = console; set to a file's FCB during PRINT#/INPUT#/file I/O, reset to 0 at end of a console PRINT. Default $0000.
label_comments.0867: [RE] Saved current text/statement pointer (MS BASIC SAVTXT): the running program pointer loaded into HL to execute; set to $FFFF in direct mode; CONT checks ==$FFFF for 'no continue'. Default $FFFE. Loaded/saved across CONT/RESUME and the storage-overflow guard.
label_comments.0869: Start of BASIC program text (MS BASIC TXTTAB): base of the linked line list, scanned by FNDLIN/relink/RUN/CLEAR. Default $84C9. The ,P-protected DECODE runs $0846..$0B6F.
label_comments.0871: [RE] Seed pointer for slot 0 of the file/FOR-slot pointer array at $0850: cold-start loads it with the warm-start re-entry MAIN_LOOP_ENTRY_1 ($81D1) and the CHAIN/OPEN reinit copies $084E into $0850/$0840 after CLEAR_VARS. Effectively the default first-slot/command pointer.
label_comments.0873: [RE] File/FOR-slot pointer array (MS BASIC FILTAB), indexed by max-open-files ($0870); each entry points at an FCB base. Slot 0 doubles as the deferred start-up command pointer consulted at the Ok/main-loop entry ($81D1). 126-byte region.
label_comments.08F2: [RE] CRUNCH tokenizer line work buffer (MS BASIC KBUF): 318-byte zero-init RAM area; CRUNCH loads DE=$08CF as the destination where the crunched/tokenized line is built (and the LIST de-tokenizer reuses it). NOT the reserved-word name table (those strings live at $025E-$04D7; $04D8 is the operator sub-table).
label_comments.0A31: Console line-input buffer (MS BASIC BUF): INLIN reads/echoes the edited input line here; Ctrl-U/Ctrl-R reset the pointer to $0A0E. Buffer body continues through $0A1E.
label_comments.0B46: Top-of-storage / highest usable RAM pointer (MS BASIC MEMSIZ): cold-start sets it below the stack; GETSTK checks SP vs MEMSIZ; the string heap top ($0B48) seeds from it. Read by GARBAG.
label_comments.0B6B: [RE] Top-of-free-string-space pointer (MS BASIC FRETOP): the string heap allocates downward from here; GETSPA decrements it, FRESTR1 hands back the topmost string, GARBAG slides live strings up to it. Seeded from MEMSIZ at cold-start/CLEAR.
label_comments.0B73: [RE] Saved text pointer of the current DATA line during a READ ($3A5C stores the located DATA line ptr). On a READ-time parse error STMT_LINE_1 restores it into SAVTXT ($0D69 -> $0844) so '? ... in <line>' reports against the DATA line, not the READ line.
label_comments.0B7A: [RE] AUTO line-numbering mode flag: AUTO sets it nonzero ($36F9) with start/increment in $0B58/$0B5A; the READY/line dispatcher checks it to auto-generate the next line-number prompt and clears it on completion/overflow; CLEAR_VARS zeroes it.
label_comments.0B7B: [RE] AUTO current/next line number: set by the AUTO command ($36FD) and advanced by $0B5A each input; the line dispatcher uses it to prompt/insert the next auto-numbered line.
label_comments.0B7D: [RE] AUTO line-number increment (default $000A): set by the AUTO command ($36F6) and added to AUTLIN ($0B58) by the line dispatcher to form the next auto line number.
label_comments.0B7F: [RE] Saved text pointer of the current statement (MS BASIC OLDTXT): NEWSTT records it each statement ($3374); STOP/END copies it to the CONT save ($0B6D at $6979); error/'Redo from start' restores from it.
label_comments.0B81: [RE] Saved stack pointer (MS BASIC SAVSTK): written via LD ($0B5E),SP at NEWSTT ($3377) and cold-start; restored to SP on error-recovery / FOR-stack unwinding (e.g. STMT_CALL_6 $72AF) to discard pending expression frames.
label_comments.0B83: [RE] Error-handler saved text pointer: ERROR_DISPATCH stores SAVTXT ($0844) here ($0D8C); the message printer reads it ($0DFC/$0E00) to decide direct vs '? ... in <line>'; RESUME reloads it into SAVTXT ($36B5).
label_comments.0B85: [RE] Saved program line of the last error (MS BASIC ERRLIN): ERROR_DISPATCH records the offending line ($0D9B) for ERR/ERL reporting; LINGET '.' shortcut substitutes it as the current line number ($34D9).
label_comments.0B8B: [RE] ON-ERROR trap-active flag (MS BASIC ONEFLG): nonzero while inside an error handler; gates ON-ERROR dispatch ($3663), RESUME ($368C), and blocks CONT ('Can't continue' at $0D2E); cleared by CLEAR ($68BE).
label_comments.0B8C: [RE] FRMEVL operand text-pointer scratch (general TEMP): the precedence loop saves/reloads the current (HL) here ($3A85/$3A88) across operator recursion. The same cell is reused by FOUT to record the decimal-point buffer position during numeric formatting.
label_comments.0B92: [RE] Start of variable + free space (MS BASIC VARTAB/STREND-grow pointer): base of the simple-variable table walked by PTRGET/CHAIN; the string-relocation copy grows the pool here; CLEAR re-points it just above program text. ,P-protected DECODE ends at $0B6F.
label_comments.0CBD: [RE] CHAIN/ON-ERROR 'preserve variables' flag: CHAIN-with-ALL and CHAIN set it ($72BA/$72C7) so the CLEAR storage reset skips clearing variable space ($6894 test); ERROR_DISPATCH clears it ($0D90); cold-start zeroes it.
label_comments.0CC3: [RE] CHAIN-in-progress / break-pause flag: set to 1 during CHAIN string-var move ($752C) so the CLEAR reset preserves the string heap ($68CC test); also the Ctrl-C/list-pause flag polled by the auto-page LIST 'more' handler ($673B). Cleared by ERROR_DISPATCH and cold-start.
label_comments.14E4: [RE] CHRGET then GETINT-positive: advances the text pointer, evaluates an expr to a signed 16-bit int (via SUB_34CC); used where a leading char must be skipped first (e.g. WIDTH/coord parsers at $60DD/$60F5/$7F41/$7F7F)
label_comments.14E7: [RE] GETINT requiring a non-negative result: CALL GETINT; RET P if the high byte (D) is sign-positive (0..$7FFF), else fall into SUB_34CC_1 which loads E=$05 and JP $0D89 -> 'Illegal function call' (FC). Widely used by graphics/coord and array parsers
label_comments.1BE4: [RE] FRMEVL operator-apply coercion (mis-split as DEFB, real code reached from FRMEVL_OPCOMBINE $3B76 when operand-type B==$04): CALL FN_CINT to force the operand to integer, then fall into FRMEVL_OP_POP_FRAME
label_comments.1BE7: [RE] FRMEVL operator-apply (mis-split DEFB code, target of JP Z at $3B63 for string type): POP BC / POP DE to recover the operator/operand frame, then fall into FRMEVL_OP_DISPATCH_REL
label_comments.1BE9: [RE] FRMEVL operator-apply tail (mis-split DEFB code): LD HL,$050D (relational/string-op handler vector base) then JR back into the operator-result combine loop at ~$3BA9 to dispatch the pending operator
label_comments.1CE8: Fold the char already in A from ASCII lowercase a-z ($61-$7A) to uppercase (AND $5F); other chars unchanged. Entry to CHRGET_UPCASE that skips the LD A,(HL); used by CRUNCH keyword matching and the &H/&O scanner
label_comments.1CF6: [RE] '&' radix-literal scanner (FRMEVL reaches it at $3C24 on token $26): parses &H<hex> (ADD HL,HL x4 + nibble) and &O<octal> (ADD HL,HL x3 + digit) into HL, stores as an integer in the FAC via FP_STORE_FAC_INT; Overflow (E=$06,$0D81) on too many digits, Syntax ($0D6F) on a bad octal digit
label_comments.1DF3: [RE] Integer-operands binary-operator handler (mis-split as DEFB, real code; set up by FRMEVL_OPLOOP_13 at $3B31 LD BC,$3DD8): pops operator token in A and the two integer operands (DE,HL), branches per token to integer add/sub/AND/OR/XOR/relational kernels, leaving the integer result in the FAC
label_comments.23F3: Data string 'Undefined line ' (+NUL) printed by the RENUM/line-reference checker (loaded at $43C1, emitted via STROUT) before the offending line number
label_comments.24A6: Data string 'Random number seed (-32768- to 32767)' (with a $08 backspace splice) -- the interactive RANDOMIZE prompt emitted by STMT_RANDOMIZE via STROUT/QINLIN
label_comments.2D79: [RE] 16x16 unsigned multiply for array subscript/offset computation (callers in PTRGET array code $61BB/$61FD): HL_running*BC accumulated by 16-iteration shift-add into DE; on overflow JP PTRGET_SEARCH_27 -> 'Subscript out of range' (E=$09). Result in DE
label_comments.3210: [RE] Shared 'DEC A; RET' counter helper: decrements the digit/exponent count in A. Tail of FIN_MUL10 and called conditionally by the FOUT exponent formatter ($5986)
label_comments.3856: [RE] Double-precision 0.5 rounding-bias FP constant: DADD'd to the value before FIX in FN_LPOS ($4F86) and in the fractional-digit generator ($5B05) to round-to-nearest
label_comments.385A: [RE] Single-precision 0.5 rounding constant loaded by FADD_LOAD_CONST/FADDT ($4B98, $5C90): added to the FAC during single-precision round-before-truncate in FOUT/FIX
label_comments.385E: [RE] FP magnitude threshold constant compared via DCOMP in the FOUT exponent path ($593C): the value above which numeric output switches to E (scientific) notation
label_comments.3866: [RE] Double-precision power-of-ten (negative-exponent) table used by FOUT_DIGITS_FRAC ($5B14): each fractional decimal digit extracted by repeated subtraction of these scaled-ten constants from the FAC fraction
label_comments.38AC: [RE] Second double-precision power-of-ten constant block for fractional-digit generation (loaded as DE at $5B55 in the FOUT fraction loop), companion to the $5BE8 table
label_comments.38B2: [RE] Little-endian integer power-of-ten table 10000/1000/100/10/1 ($10 27 / E8 03 / 64 00 / 0A 00 / 01 00) used by FOUT_DIGITS_INT ($5B8C): each integer decimal digit produced by repeated subtraction from the FAC mantissa
label_comments.3AA2: [RE] RND seed (RNDX) work cell, low byte of the 4-byte running random state at $5E24-$5E27. CLEAR/RUN re-initializes it from a default constant ($68AE FP_MOVE4 from $5D85); FN_RND multiplies/updates it; also reused as FP scratch by FN_SQR ($5DA3/$5DCC/$5DF4)
label_comments.3AA3: [RE] RND seed mantissa word (upper 3 bytes of the RNDX state at $5E25-$5E27): RANDOMIZE stores the new seed here via STMT_RANDOMIZE_3 ($4483 LD ($5E25),HL)
label_comments.48C1: [RE] Print a string VALUE: free the temp descriptor (FRESTR), load its address->BC and length->D (FP_LOAD_MEM3), then output D bytes via OUTCHR, resetting print state on CR. Entry past STROUT's literal scan; used by PRINT/LPRINT/INPUT-prompt ($37C4/$3927/$65EF/$75B4/$75D6)
label_comments.608D: Data string ' Bytes free'+CRLF -- free-memory suffix printed after the byte count in the cold sign-on banner (loaded at $83DF, emitted via STROUT)
label_comments.609B: Data string: leading sign-on banner text (CRLF CRLF CRLF then 'BASIC-80 ...'), printed first by COLD_SIGNON ($83D5 LD HL,$841D / STROUT) ahead of the free-bytes count
label_comments.0100: CP/M .COM entry point at $0100: JP COLD_START ($5E51). MBASIC runs in place (no relocation), so this is the only fixed CP/M load address. Replaces SUB_0100. [RE]
label_comments.0300: Interior continuation label inside the reserved-word name table (RESLST, ~$0522-$04D7 statement names plus per-keyword dispatch DEFW that CRUNCH folds to tokens). Not a routine; the FOUT reference at $34DE (LD BC,$0300) is an immediate-constant coincidence, not a real pointer into the table. Was L_0300. [RE]
label_comments.033E: Interior continuation label inside the reserved-word name table (RESLST keyword-name + token-dispatch table). Holds keyword spelling bytes ('TAB','SCRN','EX$'...). The FN_LOF reference at $52FA (LD BC,$033E) is an immediate-constant coincidence, not a table pointer. Was L_033E. [RE]
label_comments.0CB6: Editor/LIST working flag (MBASIC equiv of GBASIC's $0C93; the low-RAM cells shift +$23 between the two builds). INPUT_PROMPT_SEP stores 0 or ';' here; INLIN_CR_FINISH tests it to choose auto-redisplay of a prefilled buffer vs normal line terminate; DETOKENIZE_LINE clears it as the inter-token spacing state. Was L_0CB6. [RE]
label_comments.0CB7: INPUT '?'-prompt suppression flag (MBASIC equiv of GBASIC's $0C94, +$23 shift). INPUT_PROMPT sets $FF (emit '? '); a trailing ',' clears it to 0 to suppress the question mark. INPUT_EMIT_PROMPT tests it. Was L_0CB7. [RE]
label_comments.0CB8: CHAIN string-compaction temp: CHAIN_COMPACT_STRINGS saves FRETOP (top-of-string) here ($5188) and restores it ($51D5) around the string-heap move that preserves COMMON variables. MBASIC equiv of GBASIC's $0C95 (+$23 shift). Was L_0CB8. [RE]
label_comments.0CBA: Default file record length / random-record limit cell. COLD_SET_WIDTH inits it to $0080 (128, the CP/M record size); FILE_NUM_TO_FCB range-checks the requested record number against it ($5BC4) and file setup re-stores it ($5FC7/$5FF8). MBASIC equiv of GBASIC's open-file-table-limit cell $0C97 (+$23 shift); the stale '$0CBA' in the FP comments is a transferred GBASIC note (real FP ARG temp is $0CDD). Was L_0CBA. [RE]
label_comments.0CBC: 'Running a stored program' / protected-program flag (MBASIC equiv of GBASIC's $0C99, +$23 shift). COLD_START and CLEAR_VARS clear it; ILLEGAL_DIRECT_CHECK ($5E2B) tests it (zero => Illegal direct error). LOAD/MERGE sets it to $FE (the SAVE,P protected marker) then triggers PROG_SCRAMBLE. Was L_0CBC. [RE]
label_comments.064E: Interior label inside the error-message text table ($0522-$07xx); points mid-string at 'defined user functi...' (tail of the FN/undefined-function message). Not code. The lone JR-target reference is itself a mis-disassembled string byte. Was SUB_064E. [RE]
label_comments.0752: Interior label inside the error-message text table; points at 'ber\0File not found' (end of '...number' + 'File not found'). String data, not a routine. Was SUB_0752. [RE]
label_comments.0925: Cell in the zero-init file-control / FCB scratch RAM region (between FILTAB_20 at $0920 and the BUF area); part of the per-file FCB/buffer workspace. The DEFW SUB_0925 at $019D is a statement-dispatch-table misalignment artifact (table is even-aligned from $0108; $019D is odd), not a real handler pointer. Was SUB_0925. [RE]
label_comments.0B2A: Start of the console/printer terminal-position work-cell block ($0B2A-$0B45, preceding MEMSIZ). The heavily-used cell at +$0A ($0B34, ex SUB_0B2A_2) is the current print column (TTYPOS), with adjacent width/comma-zone/printer-position and CHRGET pointer cells ($0B3A/$0B3C). Was SUB_0B2A. [RE]
label_comments.0C03: Start of a zero-init runtime work-cell block ($0C03-$0C4A) in the dynamic-storage area past VARTAB. Holds FRMEVL/DEF-FN expression temporaries (e.g. the DEF-FN saved-value cell at $0C1E, ex SUB_0C03_2) and a pointer slot inited from VARTAB_5 at COLD_SET_WIDTH ($5F1C). Was SUB_0C03. [RE]
label_comments.0C4B: Start of a zero-init runtime work-cell block ($0C4B-$0C97) holding variable/array/temp-string-descriptor and OPTION/PRINT-USING work cells (e.g. CHAIN size temp at $0C88 ex SUB_0C4B_5; OPTION-BASE flag at $0C97 ex SUB_0C4B_13). Was SUB_0C4B. [RE]
label_comments.0D04: Cell in the zero-init RAM work area following the CHAIN flag cells ($0D00-region); part of the direct-mode/error scratch state. Reached only as data (no real code reference). Was SUB_0D04. [RE]
label_comments.0F26: Continuation in PROG_LINE_INSERT (insert/replace a tokenized program line): stores the next-line link pointer into the spliced line before the body copy. Was SUB_0F26. [RE]
label_comments.0F28: PROG_LINE_INSERT splice loop: copies the crunched line (BC bytes from the $08F2 line buffer) up into the gap opened in program memory, then CHEAD-relinks. Was SUB_0F28. [RE]
label_comments.1128: Continuation of the CRUNCH special-token check (reached from CRUNCH_RESWORD_TAIL_9 at $1103 via PUSH BC/RET): a CP <token>/RET Z chain testing the just-emitted token against the set that gets special crunch handling (line-number-taking/no-space tokens like GOTO/GOSUB/THEN/ELSE etc.). Was SUB_1128. [RE]
label_comments.1402: CHRGOT embedded-token continuation: on the $10-class constant token, reload the saved text pointer (TERM_POS_WORK+$10, $0B3A) and re-enter CHRGOT. Part of the in-place CHRGET/CHRGOT char-fetch + constant-token expansion ($13E0 area). Was SUB_1402. [RE]
label_comments.1E1D: Continuation of FRMEVL_INT_OP_HANDLER (integer binary-operator dispatch): tests the operator token for XOR ($3C) and computes HL := HL XOR DE, else falls through to EQV/IMP. Was SUB_1E1D. [RE]
label_comments.1E5A: USR(n) function call dispatch: after USRVEC_ADDR/FRMEVL_PAREN, fetch the USR vector address from the DEF-USR table and set up the call (push return, load arg type from $0B37), entering the user routine. Was SUB_1E5A. [RE]
label_comments.242B: Taken branch of RENUM_FIXUP_IF_PENDING: when the renumber-pending flag ($0B79) is set, JP RENUM_PATCH_LINEREFS to run the line-reference fix-up pass. Was SUB_242B. [RE]
label_comments.2803: Disk-error exit helper reached from the coded-error stubs at $27F5+: save the error code, issue BDOS Select-Disk (C=$0E) using the CP/M current-drive byte at $0004 to reselect the default drive, then JP ERROR_DISPATCH. Was SUB_2803. [RE]
label_comments.2922: Loop back of the bit-granular mantissa right-shift (MANT_SHIFT_BITS): JR to MANT_SHIFT_BYTES_4 to continue rotating C,D,E,B right one bit per step during FP exponent alignment in add/subtract. Was SUB_2922. [RE]
label_comments.2D48: Continuation of FIX_DENORM (FIX/INT toward-integer denormalize): after FP_LOAD_FAC, unpack the FAC MSB (FP_UNPACK_MSB), force exponent $B8 and right-shift the mantissa to drop the fractional bits. Was SUB_2D48. [RE]
label_comments.2FB4: Loop body of DP_SHIFT_LEFT_8: chained RLA across the 8 mantissa bytes from (HL), one bit left, used between partial products in the double-precision multiply (also CALLed at $30DC). Was SUB_2FB4. [RE]
label_comments.3248: FIN numeric-input accumulation step: HL := HL*10 + new digit (the ADD HL,BC after the *10 shift chain), accumulating an integer constant during string-to-FP conversion; overflow promotes to single (INT_TO_SINGLE). Was SUB_3248. [RE]
label_comments.3415: PRINT USING / FOUT field formatter: prepend a leading '$' ($24) to the formatted number buffer when the field flag bit $10 (floating-dollar) is set. Was SUB_3415. [RE]
label_comments.3D15: Success-exit of PTRGET_SEARCH (variable lookup): variable matched, restore HL (descriptor pointer already in place) and RET. Was SUB_3D15. [RE]
label_comments.3D4E: Inner loop of the array subscript/dimension evaluation (PTRGET array path): reads BC word-pairs of subscript/dimension values and PUSHes them, decrementing the count, before computing the element address. The DEFW at $0165 referencing it is a dispatch-table misalignment artifact. Was SUB_3D4E. [RE]
label_comments.43B7: INCHR continuation after a file-EOF triggers LOAD_FINISH_CLOSE_CUR: restore DE/BC, test the CHAIN flag, and on a normal console read fall through to the break/auto-page handling. Was SUB_43B7. [RE]
label_comments.4508: CLEAR_VARS continuation: store the computed program-end as the new VARTAB (variable-table base), then fall into CLEAR_RESET_DATAPTR to rebuild the storage map. Was SUB_4508. [RE]
label_comments.453A: Continuation of CLEAR_RESET_STORAGE (NEW/CLEAR/RUN reinit): zero the math/temp pointer cells, ONEFLG, ERRLIN and (when not CHAINing) reseed FRETOP from MEMSIZ, then RESTORE the DATA pointer. Was SUB_453A. [RE]
label_comments.45A8: Match path of SYNCHR (the 'expect this inline token' primitive): the current char equalled the literal byte after the CALL, so step past it (EX (SP),HL / INC HL), CHRGOT the next char and RET to the caller past the inline literal. Was SUB_45A8. [RE]
label_comments.4845: Body of STR_BUILD_FROM_DESC (allocate+copy a string from a descriptor): after reading the length byte, GETSPA the space, fetch the source pointer, STORE_STR_DESC, and BLOCK_COPY the bytes into the new heap body. Was SUB_4845. [RE]
label_comments.4860: Store-string-descriptor core with HL pre-loaded to the target descriptor cell: writes length A and body pointer DE at (HL), returns HL. STORE_STR_DESC ($485D) enters here with HL = temp-descriptor $0B68; also CALLed directly ($1006). Was SUB_4860. [RE]
label_comments.494C: GARBAG (string garbage collection) scan-advance: ADD HL,DE to step the scan pointer past the current variable/array entry, then loop back to GARBAG_4. Was SUB_494C. [RE]
label_comments.49C3: Stack-reordering tail of GARBAG_FIX_STR_PTR: after selecting the highest in-use string descriptor, POP/PUSH-juggle the saved registers and RET with the chosen pointer. Was SUB_49C3. [RE]
label_comments.4C03: CHAIN COMMON variable-copy continuation: build/relocate a string body (STR_BUILD_FROM_DESC) or move a typed numeric value (FP_MOVE_TYPED) for a variable being preserved across the program reload. Was SUB_4C03. [RE]
label_comments.4CA1: INLIN editor state reset (entered just below INLIN_PUT_AND_RESET): clear the pending-control self-mod byte ($0857) and the INLIN_REDISPLAY_FLAG ($0CB6) before reading a fresh input line. Was SUB_4CA1. [RE]
label_comments.5012: STMT_CHAIN COMMON-list continuation: test whether the just-parsed variable name is followed by '(' ($28) to mark it as an array vs scalar before flagging it (high-bit) for preservation across CHAIN. Was SUB_5012. [RE]
label_comments.5226: STMT_WRITE continuation: after FOUT-ing a numeric item, strip the leading space FOUT inserts (WRITE emits no leading blank) by shifting the descriptor and decrementing its length, then STRPRT the value. Was SUB_5226. [RE]
label_comments.552B: CLOSE-statement dispatch trampoline: PUSH BC/RET to jump to the per-file close handler (FILE_CLOSE_ONE) with the close-all loop continuation already stacked. Was SUB_552B. [RE]
label_comments.5A3B: STMT_OPEN continuation: after the BDOS open/create, zero the FCB working fields at FCB+$25 (current-record / extent / random-record cells) so the new channel starts at record 0. Was SUB_5A3B. [RE]
label_comments.5B25: STMT_FILES (directory listing) per-entry continuation: echo the filename character (OUTCHR), optionally space/CRLF for column layout, then issue BDOS Search-Next (C=$12) for the next directory match. Was SUB_5B25. [RE]
label_comments.5D0D: Continuation inside FIELD_WRITE_RECORD (random-file PUT FIELD-chain walk): pop the saved FCB+$AB pointer, store the advanced write cursor, and on buffer-full branch to dispatch the record write else return via the epilogue. Was SUB_5D0D. [RE]
label_comments.5D26: Common register-restore epilogue of FIELD_WRITE_RECORD (POP HL/DE/BC; RET). The DEFW at $019F referencing it is a statement-dispatch-table misalignment artifact, not a real handler. Was SUB_5D26. [RE]
label_comments.5E27: Exit of DIRECT_MODE_GUARD: pop the saved HL, INC A on the SAVTXT high-byte AND result and RET NZ (i.e. allow when a stored line is active). Shared tail just above ILLEGAL_DIRECT_CHECK. Was SUB_5E27. [RE]
label_comments.SUB_1A90: [RE] Bare expression-evaluator entry (no leading '(' required): DEC HL to back up, then fall into the FRMEVL operator-precedence loop with D=0. The general 'evaluate expression at (HL) into FAC' call used throughout the parsers (60+ sites).
label_comments.SUB_1CF1: [RE] Two-way operand guard: if the current char is '&' ($26) fall into the &H/&O radix-constant scanner, else JP LINGET to parse a decimal line number.
label_comments.SUB_2BED: [RE] Double-precision relational comparator (OPERATOR_ROUTINE_TBL double-compare slot $050B): CALL DCOMP_BODY then collapse the result to A=-1/0/+1 (FP_SIGN_1+1) -- the double analog of FCOMP.
label_comments.SUB_2DC1: [RE] 16-bit signed integer multiply (OPERATOR_ROUTINE_TBL integer-multiply slot $051B): sign-normalize then a 16-iteration shift-and-add of BC into HL; on overflow promote both operands to single and re-enter FMUL.
label_comments.SUB_52F8: [RE] CVI() function handler (FUNC_DISPATCH_TBL slot $0204; CVS/CVD enter at +offset with widths 2/4/8): free the argument string temp (FRETMP), check it is wide enough (else FC), reinterpret its bytes as a numeric and load the FAC.
label_comments.SUB_56C8: [RE] EOF() function handler (FUNC_DISPATCH_TBL slot $020C): resolve the file number to its FCB and return the end-of-file boolean.
label_comments.L_0577: [RE] Error message string "Overflow" (error $06): loaded by the error reporter; the overflow trap also sets the current-message pointer ($0848) to it.
label_comments.L_05D0: [RE] Error message string "Division by zero" (error $0B): the FP divide-by-zero path stores this pointer into the current-error-message cell ($0848).
